source.subject: Repeated eval credit requests — account credential reuse?
messages:
From: Eli Torres <eli@scalebridge.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: additional eval credits for pilot

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the help during our trial. We're in the middle of an internal legal review and multiple people on our engineering and ML teams are trying to finish integration tests. Several of our engineers have hit the credit cap for isolated pilots and are asking for short-term top-ups so we can complete a consolidated evaluation next week.

Can Redwood issue another set of evaluation credits or extend the trial window for ScaleBridge (account id SB-993)? We've had people request credits under different emails (I can list them) because teams were testing isolated flows. We want to avoid losing momentum.

If there are any compliance checks you need from our side, let me know — happy to provide a point of contact.

Best,
Eli Torres
Head of ML
ScaleBridge

---
Attachment: none
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Eli Torres <eli@scalebridge.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T10:01:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: additional eval credits for pilot

Hi Eli —

Appreciate the context. To move quickly I need a short list of the accounts/emails that requested credits (and whether they belong to ScaleBridge employees). Also can you confirm whether any of these were invited by contractors or external partners? We do occasional one-time top-ups for coordinated pilot teams but we need to validate against our abuse patterns first.

Jordan, looping you in to see if there are open deals or recent bulk signups for SB-993.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

---
From: Eli Torres <eli@scalebridge.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Olivia Park <olivia.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: additional eval credits for pilot

Ava —

Thanks. List below — all are employees at ScaleBridge:
- eli@scalebridge.com
- olivia.park@scalebridge.com
- samir.khan@scalebridge.com
- tegan.li@scalebridge.com
- infra@scalebridge.com (shared service account)

Samir and Tegan started separate small pilots but are on the same engineering org. infra@ is our automation account used for CI replay jobs. We only want a short, one-week extension or a one-time credit grant to finish integration validation.

Happy to sign an addendum if required.

Best,
Eli

---
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: additional eval credits for pilot — suspected credential reuse?

Marissa — forwarding ScaleBridge thread. At first glance this looks like a normal pilot request, except our request history shows a cluster of credit top-ups tied to the same billing fingerprint but coming from 5 distinct emails over the last 10 days. I attached a quick snapshot.

Observations:
- Multiple extra requests from accounts that map to SB-993 but with overlapping IPs/UA strings (attached).
- infra@scalebridge.com is used for CI and shows repeated automated sessions that consumed tokens overnight.
- No prior exemption on file for bulk/team grants under this account.

Options:
1) Approve a one-time, limited credit grant (e.g., 20k tokens) after manual verification and require a signed pilot addendum.
2) Deny and offer a paid short-term pilot (discounted) and help them migrate the infra runs to a paid org quota.

We need fraud/abuse perspective before we issue more free credits.

--
Jordan Blake
Sales | Redwood Inference
Attachment: scalebridge_request_history.csv; scalebridge_user_agent_snapshot.txt
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: additional eval credits for pilot — suspected credential reuse?

Team — thanks for the signals and the quick triage. Given the overlapping fingerprints and the infra account behaviour, I want to be conservative here: we should not blanket-issue additional complimentary credits without tighter controls.

Action items (owner in parens):
- Send a templated response to ScaleBridge offering a one-week limited credit grant (20k tokens) conditioned on: 1) a signed pilot addendum; 2) confirmation that infra@ is a company-managed service account and not a recycled/shared credential; 3) a list of employee IDs for the five requesters (Ava).
- Marissa: please log the file in abuse-tracker and mark as monitored; include the UA/IP snapshot in the incident.
- Jordan: prepare a commercial alternative (two-week discounted pilot price) to present if they decline the addendum.

We also need to update the deal record (HB-4582) with this decision and the attachments. If they provide the requested confirmation and addendum, we can approve the one-time credit within 24 hours. If anything on their verification looks inconsistent, deny and route them to paid pilot.

I'll take ownership of any escalations from legal.

Rishi Malhotra
Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com

---
Attachment: excerpt_redwood_eval_credit_policy.pdf

source.subject: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@medaxis.io>, Tom Brewer <tom.brewer@medaxis.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Hi Elena / Tom —

Thanks again for the deep dive last week. MedAxis is ready to move forward with the Dedicated deployment but needs a pricing structure that matches their constrained budget for H1. They asked for a one-off concession to make the pilot and regulatory work economical: 45% off list for an 18-month committed package (dedicated GPU pool + 24/7 support + VPC egress allowance) and onboarding fees waived.

High level:
- Expected baseline monthly spend: ~$120k list (tokens + seats + infra)
- Requested concession: 45% off list, onboarding waiver
- Term: 18 months, auto-renew unless flagged
- Strategic ask from MedAxis: compliance proofs and a private-control plane pilot (drives their timeline)

I'm asking for internal signoff from Sales leadership / Finance to route this to legal. Jordan, can you run a quick margin sensitivity and call out the downside at 45%? Marissa, flagging for VP/CFO signoff — they are pushing to close this quarter.

Attachments: preliminary quote (I'll attach the pricing sheet in the follow-up).

Next steps I propose:
1) Margin calc from Revenue Ops (Jordan) by EOD tomorrow
2) If economics are within tolerance, Marissa to escalate to Markus for CFO approval
3) I will send a tailored mutual action plan + draft addendum for legal

Would appreciate an expedited review — MedAxis expects an answer this week or they move to an alternate vendor.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Alex —

Thanks for the context. Quick read: MedAxis is strategic and the pilot has good reference potential, but a 45% flat concession for 18 months is large and will need CFO signoff. Jordan, please prioritize a one-page sensitivity that shows:
- Annualized ARR under list vs requested concession
- Gross margin impact (absolute $ and %) over 18 months
- Upside scenarios (usage overrun) and walkaway breakpoints

Also include proposed guardrails to limit downside (minimum commit floors, overage rates, performance milestones). If we can't quantify mitigations, I won't escalate a 45% concession.

Alex — think about a structured concession (stepped discount, usage-based rebate, or milestone unlocks) that preserves some margin while giving the customer the headline number. If you have a draft MAP or DocuSign template, include it in the follow-up.

I'll hold on escalation until Jordan sends the numbers. Markus, flagging you as the finance approver; happy to route to you for a decision once we have the sensitivity.

-Marissa
VP Sales, Commercial
Redwood Inference

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Marissa / Alex —

Attached are the worksheets: MedAxis_MA_Pricing_Sheet.xlsx and margin_sensitivity.csv. Summary below.

Summary (18-month view):
- List revenue (projected): $2,160,000
- Revenue @ 45% concession: $1,188,000 (delta -$972,000)
- Current gross margin (typical for this bundle): ~55%
- Projected gross margin @ 45% concession (no mitigations): ~28%
- Contribution margin drop: ~27 percentage points (~$263k reduction in gross profit over 18 months)

Mitigation scenarios modeled:
A) Stepped discount: 35% (months 1-6) -> 25% (months 7-18): lifts gross margin to ~40%
B) Usage floor + overage: Minimum commitment floor at 80% of expected tokens; overage at list rates above floor -> lifts margin to ~44% if utilization holds
C) Performance milestone unlock: additional 10% rebate contingent on achieving X API latency / uptime targets and 12-month consumption thresholds (transfers risk to customer)

My recommendation: Prefer A + B combined. That yields a tolerable margin loss and gives Salesteam the ability to market a headline discount while protecting downside.

Notes: all numbers assume current kernel costs and reserved GPU pricing; if hardware spot prices change, we should re-run.

-Jordan
Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment stubs: MedAxis_MA_Pricing_Sheet.xlsx (pricing tabs, cost model), margin_sensitivity.csv (scenario outputs)
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@medaxis.io>, Tom Brewer <tom.brewer@medaxis.io>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Marissa / Jordan / Markus —

Thanks Jordan — useful sensitivity and very clear. Based on A + B (stepped discount + minimum floor) I discussed a compromise with MedAxis:
- Offer: 35% off list months 1-6, 25% off list months 7-18
- Minimum committed monthly spend floor at 80% of projected tokens (otherwise true-up)
- Onboarding fee waived conditional on DocuSign of MAP within 10 business days
- Performance unlock: if MedAxis exceeds 120% of projected usage for any 3-month rolling period, we offer a one-time credit (to avoid unlimited upside leakage)

I've uploaded a draft MAP and the proposed addendum here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1G-MedAxis-MAP (DocuSign link included in related_links).

If Sales Leadership + Finance can greenlight this banded concession, I'll send the legal-ready addendum and ask MedAxis to sign. Time is tight; they want an executable by 10/29.

Alex
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Oct 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Team —

I reviewed Jordan's sensitivity and Alex's revised commercial. Finance approves a constrained concession with the following conditions:

1) Approved commercial: 35% off list for months 1-6, 25% off list for months 7-18 (as proposed).
2) Minimum monthly spend floor at 80% and explicit true-up clause; overages billed at list rates.
3) Onboarding fee waived only if MAP and SOW are signed within 10 business days of countersignature.
4) Add a 12-month clawback clause in the addendum: if cumulative utilization is <65% of committed tokens at 12 months, partial rebate reversals apply.
5) Legal to include the approved pricing addendum; Finance must receive the signed addendum before revenue recognition.

I've attached the signed pricing addendum (approved_pricing_addendum-signed.pdf) for records and will update HubSpot with an approved deal stage and finance note. Deal ID: DEAL-2026-1189.

Marking this Approved -> proceed to Legal and Customer signature.

-Markus Klein
VP Finance
Redwood Inference

Attachment stub: approved_pricing_addendum-signed.pdf

Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@medaxis.io>, Tom Brewer <tom.brewer@medaxis.io>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Marissa / Jordan / Markus —

Thanks Jordan — useful sensitivity and very clear. Based on A + B (stepped discount + minimum floor) I discussed a compromise with MedAxis:
- Offer: 35% off list months 1-6, 25% off list months 7-18
- Minimum committed monthly spend floor at 80% of projected tokens (otherwise true-up)
- Onboarding fee waived conditional on DocuSign of MAP within 10 business days
- Performance unlock: if MedAxis exceeds 120% of projected usage for any 3-month rolling period, we offer a one-time credit (to avoid unlimited upside leakage)

I've uploaded a draft MAP and the proposed addendum here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1G-MedAxis-MAP (DocuSign link included in related_links).

If Sales Leadership + Finance can greenlight this banded concession, I'll send the legal-ready addendum and ask MedAxis to sign. Time is tight; they want an executable by 10/29.

Alex
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

I reviewed Jordan's sensitivity and Alex's revised commercial. Finance approves a constrained concession with the following conditions:

1) Approved commercial: 35% off list for months 1-6, 25% off list for months 7-18 (as proposed).

Subject: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Hi Elena / Tom —

Thanks again for the deep dive last week. MedAxis is ready to move forward with the Dedicated deployment but needs a pricing structure that matches their constrained budget for H1. They asked for a one-off concession to make the pilot and regulatory work economical: 45% off list for an 18-month committed package (dedicated GPU pool + 24/7 support + VPC egress allowance) and onboarding fees waived.

High level:
- Expected baseline monthly spend: ~$120k list (tokens + seats + infra)
- Requested concession: 45% off list, onboarding waiver
- Term: 18 months, auto-renew unless flagged
- Strategic ask from MedAxis: compliance proofs and a private-control plane pilot (drives their timeline)

I'm asking for internal signoff from Sales leadership / Finance to route this to legal. Jordan, can you run a quick margin sensitivity and call out the downside at 45%? Marissa, flagging for VP/CFO signoff — they are pushing to close this quarter.

Attachments: preliminary quote (I'll attach the pricing sheet in the follow-up).

Next steps I propose:
1) Margin calc from Revenue Ops (Jordan) by EOD tomorrow
2) If economics are within tolerance, Marissa to escalate to Markus for CFO approval
3) I will send a tailored mutual action plan + draft addendum for legal

Would appreciate an expedited review — MedAxis expects an answer this week or they move to an alternate vendor.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Alex —

Thanks for the context. Quick read: MedAxis is strategic and the pilot has good reference potential, but a 45% flat concession for 18 months is large and will need CFO signoff. Jordan, please prioritize a one-page sensitivity that shows:
- Annualized ARR under list vs requested concession
- Gross margin impact (absolute $ and %) over 18 months
- Upside scenarios (usage overrun) and walkaway breakpoints

Also include proposed guardrails to limit downside (minimum commit floors, overage rates, performance milestones). If we can't quantify mitigations, I won't escalate a 45% concession.

Alex — think about a structured concession (stepped discount, usage-based rebate, or milestone unlocks) that preserves some margin while giving the customer the headline number. If you have a draft MAP or DocuSign template, include it in the follow-up.

I'll hold on escalation until Jordan sends the numbers. Markus, flagging you as the finance approver; happy to route to you for a decision once we have the sensitivity.

-Marissa
VP Sales, Commercial
Redwood Inference



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Custom bundle approval request: MedAxis high-touch capacity concession

Attached are the worksheets: MedAxis_MA_Pricing_Sheet.xlsx and margin_sensitivity.csv. Summary below.

Summary (18-month view):
- List revenue (projected): $2,160,000
- Revenue @ 45% concession: $1,188,000 (delta -$972,000)
- Current gross margin (typical for this bundle): ~55%
- Projected gross margin @ 45% concession (no mitigations): ~28%
- Contribution margin drop: ~27 percentage points (~$263k reduction in gross profit over 18 months)

Mitigation scenarios modeled:
A) Stepped discount: 35% (months 1-6) -> 25% (months 7-18): lifts gross margin to ~40%
B) Usage floor + overage: Minimum commitment floor at 80% of expected tokens; overage at list rates above floor -> lifts margin to ~44% if utilization holds
C) Performance milestone unlock: additional 10% rebate contingent on achieving X API latency / uptime targets and 12-month consumption thresholds (transfers risk to customer)

My recommendation: Prefer A + B combined. That yields a tolerable margin loss and gives Salesteam the ability to market a headline discount while protecting downside.

Notes: all numbers assume current kernel costs and reserved GPU pricing; if hardware spot prices change, we should re-run.

-Jordan
Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Clarify billing terms: tokens / characters / requests + caching impact
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>, Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Alex/Priya — thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and two clarifying asks so we can produce a short pricing sketch for your CFO.

Recap (very short):
- You run the transaction-monitoring assistant for merchant support and expect heavy read-style prompts (same templates, different variables) + some freeform investigator prompts.
- Key unknowns for our pricing sketch: monthly request volume, average length (tokens vs characters), and rough budget target. You mentioned you were trying to reconcile metrics internally (tokens vs characters vs API requests) — that’s common.

Can you confirm two things so we can produce a short decision-grade spreadsheet:
1) Ballpark monthly request range (best guess): low / mid / high (e.g., 500k / 2.5M / 5M)
2) Target spend range (monthly): e.g., $10k–$15k or $40k–$60k — whatever you’re comfortable sharing.

One quick note on caching: you mentioned ~40% of traffic might be identical prompts (templated responses). Do you mean identical full prompts and expected outputs (safe to cache whole response), or identical prompt prefixes where the variable suffix changes? The difference matters for billable tokens.

Attached: a tiny estimate workbook (fintect-monthly-estimates.xlsx) and a one-pager on how we compute tokens (redwood-1m-token-breakdown.pdf).

If it helps, I can run three scenarios and send a short 1-page recommendation before a follow-up call — would a 30-minute slot tomorrow morning (10/19 10:00am PT) work?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: fintect-monthly-estimates.xlsx)
(Attachment: redwood-1m-token-breakdown.pdf)

From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Thanks Ben — quick answers below. Appreciate the clear recap.

1) Monthly request estimate (internal convos):
- Low: ~700k requests/mo
- Mid: ~2.5M requests/mo (most likely)
- High: ~5M requests/mo (peak / holiday spikes)

2) Budget: our procurement window is tight; the initial target is $10k–$15k/month. We may expand after an ROI pilot, but that’s the starting point.

Cache detail: when I said ~40% identical, I meant the user-facing prompt template + variables are identical in ~40% cases — the outputs are usually identical or close enough that caching the template+variables would produce a correct cached response. Investigator prompts are unique.

Regarding tokens/characters: we’ve been tracking by characters in our logs; average prompt length (characters) is ~400 chars (variable text included). We don’t have tokenized numbers yet. If you can convert 400 chars -> tokens using a rough rule-of-thumb in your scenarios that would be helpful.

Happy to take a call tomorrow 10/19 10am PT — Alex and I will join.

Best,
Priya Sengupta
Head of Product
Fintect Analytics

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Monica/Jordan — forwarding Priya’s answers. Below I’ve sketched a short, decision-focused breakdown we can share with Fintect ahead of tomorrow’s call. Please review and flag any corrections. I’ll paste the short calc and recommended levers.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

(see above)

--- End forwarded message ---

Quick glossary we should align on (short):
- Tokens: unit Redwood bills on — model input tokens + model output tokens (tokenizer-dependent).
- Characters: raw character count from customer logs — not billed directly, but we can convert.
- Requests: API calls; useful for operational/throughput cost but not the billing unit we charge customers on (billing is per token).
- Prompt caching: when a request matches a cached key and Redwood can serve a stored response, that saved work can reduce billed tokens depending on how the caching path is implemented (full-response cache vs prefix reuse). If we serve a cached full response without running the model, billed tokens can be near-zero for the cached requests (we may still bill a tiny metadata or retrieval fee depending on contract). If it’s a prefix cache that only saves part of the input, billed tokens reduce proportionally to the unrepeated portion.

Baseline numbers (for a quick, conservative sketch):
- Assumptions from Priya: mid volume = 2.5M requests/month; avg prompt length ~400 chars. For conversion we’ll use a conservative rule: 1 token ~= 4 characters -> avg prompt tokens = 100 tokens.
- Avg completion (expected output) for these assistant replies = ~200 tokens.
- Total tokens per request = 300 tokens.
- Monthly billed tokens (no caching) = 300 * 2.5M = 750M tokens/month.

Caching scenario (Priya: ~40% identical full prompts):
- If we can serve full cached responses for that 40% (i.e., no model run), billed tokens drop to 60% of baseline: 0.6 * 750M = 450M tokens/month.

Cost scenarios (customer target $10k–$15k/mo):
- At a $/1M-token rate = $150 -> 450M tokens -> 450 * $150 = $67,500/month (too high).
- To hit $15k/month on 450M tokens -> implied $/1M-token = $33.3.

Practical levers to reach customer budget:
1) Increase cache hit or cache more (engineering): move more templates to full-response cache; prioritize top-20 templates that represent ~70% of identical traffic.
2) Use smaller / quantized model for templated replies (reduce per-token unit price and output size).
3) Billing construct: blended pricing for templated traffic vs freeform investigator queries (e.g., fixed per-request for cached templates + per-token for unique queries).
4) Commitment/term discount: a monthly commit or 12mo pilot gives us leverage to offer a much lower effective $/1M-token.

Proposed next steps to share with Fintect before the call:
- I’ll prepare three 1-page scenarios (Conservative / Target / Stretch) with the assumptions above and highlight which levers get them to ~$10–15k/mo.
- Recommend we surface the top-20 templates and a short export so our infra folks can estimate achievable cache hit when implemented in production.

Monica/Jordan — if that math looks sane, I’ll send the 1-pager to Priya and Alex this morning and use it as the agenda for the call.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Ben / Priya — this is perfect, thanks for running the numbers so quickly. The conservative assumptions look fine for now.

We’ll join 10/19 10:00am PT — Priya will bring the export of the top templates (should be ready in the next hour). For the call, please include a simple scenario where commitment + caching + a smaller model gets us into the $10k–$15k range. If you can show an example blended structure (e.g., $X per cached request + $Y per 1M tokens for freeform), that will be the most useful for procurement.

See you in 40m.

Alex
VP, Partnerships
Fintect Analytics
(Attachment: top-templates-export.csv)

Regarding tokens/characters: we’ve been tracking by characters in our logs; average prompt length (characters) is ~400 chars (variable text included). We don’t have tokenized numbers yet. If you can convert 400 chars -> tokens using a rough rule-of-thumb in your scenarios that would be helpful.

Happy to take a call tomorrow 10/19 10am PT — Alex and I will join.

Best,
Priya Sengupta
Head of Product
Fintect Analytics


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Monica/Jordan — forwarding Priya’s answers. Below I’ve sketched a short, decision-focused breakdown we can share with Fintect ahead of tomorrow’s call. Please review and flag any corrections. I’ll paste the short calc and recommended levers.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T18:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

(see above)

--- End forwarded message ---

Quick glossary we should align on (short):
- Tokens: unit Redwood bills on — model input tokens + model output tokens (tokenizer-dependent).
- Characters: raw character count from customer logs — not billed directly, but we can convert.
- Requests: API calls; useful for operational/throughput cost but not the billing unit we charge customers on (billing is per token).
- Prompt caching: when a request matches a cached key and Redwood can serve a stored response, that saved work can reduce billed tokens depending on how the caching path is implemented (full-response cache vs prefix reuse). If we serve a cached full response without running the model, billed tokens can be near-zero for the cached requests (we may still bill a tiny metadata or retrieval fee depending on contract). If it’s a prefix cache that only saves part of the input, billed tokens reduce proportionally to the unrepeated portion.

Baseline numbers (for a quick, conservative sketch):
- Assumptions from Priya: mid volume = 2.5M requests/month; avg prompt length ~400 chars. For conversion we’ll use a conservative rule: 1 token ~= 4 characters -> avg prompt tokens = 100 tokens.
- Avg completion (expected output) for these assistant replies = ~200 tokens.
- Total tokens per request = 300 tokens.
- Monthly billed tokens (no caching) = 300 * 2.5M = 750M tokens/month.

Caching scenario (Priya: ~40% identical full prompts):
- If we can serve full cached responses for that 40% (i.e., no model run), billed tokens drop to 60% of baseline: 0.6 * 750M = 450M tokens/month.

Cost scenarios (customer target $10k–$15k/mo):
- At a $/1M-token rate = $150 -> 450M tokens -> 450 * $150 = $67,500/month (too high).
- To hit $15k/month on 450M tokens -> implied $/1M-token = $33.3.

Practical levers to reach customer budget:
1) Increase cache hit or cache more (engineering): move more templates to full-response cache; prioritize top-20 templates that represent ~70% of identical traffic.
2) Use smaller / quantized model for templated replies (reduce per-token unit price and output size).
3) Billing construct: blended pricing for templated traffic vs freeform investigator queries (e.g., fixed per-request for cached templates + per-token for unique queries).
4) Commitment/term discount: a monthly commit or 12mo pilot gives us leverage to offer a much lower effective $/1M-token.

Proposed next steps to share with Fintect before the call:
- I’ll prepare three 1-page scenarios (Conservative / Target / Stretch) with the assumptions above and highlight which levers get them to ~$10–15k/mo.
- Recommend we surface the top-20 templates and a short export so our infra folks can estimate achievable cache hit when implemented in production.

Monica/Jordan — if that math looks sane, I’ll send the 1-pager to Priya and Alex this morning and use it as the agenda for the call.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@fintectanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Sengupta <priya@fintectanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up — quick pricing/volume clarifications + caching question

Ben / Priya — this is perfect, thanks for running the numbers so quickly. The conservative assumptions look fine for now.

We’ll join 10/19 10:00am PT — Priya will bring the export of the top templates (should be ready in the next hour). For the call, please include a simple scenario where commitment + caching + a smaller model gets us into the $10k–$15k range. If you can show an example blended structure (e.g., $X per cached request + $Y per 1M tokens for freeform), that will be the most useful for procurement.

See you in 40m.

Alex
VP, Partnerships
Fintect Analytics
(Attachment: top-templates-export.csv)

source.subject: Re: SDLC control log and secrets custody — Acme Tech SIG intake
messages:
From: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@assessco.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: security@acmetech.com
Date: 2026-10-12T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Hi Connor,

Thanks for the quick intake call this morning. For the SIG assessment we need a concise, auditable package showing: PR-level approval evidence for production deploys, CI job IDs and timestamps for the last 90 days, and a secrets custody ledger showing who requested/approved vault handoffs.

Can you provide: 
- a ledger csv (PR id, approver, timestamp, deploy target)
- sample CI run logs with job IDs (redacted build output is fine)
- notes on ephemeral credential lifecycle and where approval events are stored (link to any internal runbooks)

We'd prefer a single thread with the artifacts attached. Also let us know if anything needs redaction before we include in the assessor packet.

Emily Zhang
Security Assessor — AssessCo

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@assessco.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-13T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Emily — attaching the requested artifacts. Quick notes up front: we maintain a change-custody ledger in CSV (attached) that is generated from our PR gating system and signed off by approvers as part of the merge workflow. CI run IDs in the attached "ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt" are sampled (we can export more on request).

Attachments included: sdlc-custody-ledger-20261012.csv (redactable), ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt (sanitized build output), pr-approval-snapshots-20261013.pdf (screenshots of PR approval chain).

Action items: 
1) Emily — please confirm if the CSV needs any additional redaction (user emails vs display names).
2) Emily — confirm acceptance of the CI job ID format below (we include job IDs and timestamps, not raw build artifacts):
   - job_v2: 2026-09-15T17:08:21Z # job id 49822
   - job_v2: 2026-09-22T04:13:09Z # job id 50212

Internal note: Irene, Marissa — I included a short note on our ephemeral credential rotation (see my message below). Naomi, flag if you want me to include KMS audit links.

Connor O'Brien
Senior Compliance Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: sdlc-custody-ledger-20261012.csv; ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt; pr-approval-snapshots-20261013.pdf

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@assessco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-14T11:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Adding a quick explanatory snippet about PR gating: our merge pipeline enforces an "approval-tag" requirement on protected branches. The approval tag is generated when a user with PR-approval role signs off in the reviewer UI; that event is immutably stamped into the PR metadata and exported to the custody ledger generator. Example event row (in ledger):

PR: #2437 | repo: infra/gate | approver_role: "release-owner" | approver: "Naomi F" | ts: 2026-09-15T17:07:44Z | deploy_target: prod-us-west-2

On the CI side we store job IDs and immutability hashes in the build metadata (we don't store full logs in the ledger). The attached CI log file contains the job ids and the minimal trace we use for assessor review.

Jordan Blake
Director, Reliability Engineering

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-15T08:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Emily — a couple implementation notes to help the assessment: 
- Secrets lifecycle: ephemeral credentials are minted by our short-lived token service (SLTS). Tokens expire at 15 minutes by default. Requests and approvals are logged in /kms/audit/ and sampled into our custody ledger (display name only).
- KMS audit links (internal): https://console.redwood.internal/kms/audits?range=90d (we've added a redacted export to Drive that matches the ledger rows).
- Change management: any emergency change is recorded with an EMR tag in the ledger and requires two out-of-band exec approvals (see pr-approval-snapshots-20261013.pdf for one example).

Note on redaction: Connor attached full display names; for assessor distribution we typically replace internal emails with display names except where the assessor requires identity mapping. Happy to prepare a redacted CSV if you confirm that preference.

Irene Choi
Sr. Security PM, Platform

Date: 2026-10-16T16:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Date: 2026-10-12T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Hi Connor,

Thanks for the quick intake call this morning. For the SIG assessment we need a concise, auditable package showing: PR-level approval evidence for production deploys, CI job IDs and timestamps for the last 90 days, and a secrets custody ledger showing who requested/approved vault handoffs.

Can you provide: 
- a ledger csv (PR id, approver, timestamp, deploy target)
- sample CI run logs with job IDs (redacted build output is fine)

We'd prefer a single thread with the artifacts attached. Also let us know if anything needs redaction before we include in the assessor packet.

Emily Zhang
Security Assessor — AssessCo



From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@assessco.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-13T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Emily — attaching the requested artifacts. Quick notes up front: we maintain a change-custody ledger in CSV (attached) that is generated from our PR gating system and signed off by approvers as part of the merge workflow. CI run IDs in the attached "ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt" are sampled (we can export more on request).

Attachments included: sdlc-custody-ledger-20261012.csv (redactable), ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt (sanitized build output), pr-approval-snapshots-20261013.pdf (screenshots of PR approval chain).

Action items: 
1) Emily — please confirm if the CSV needs any additional redaction (user emails vs display names).
2) Emily — confirm acceptance of the CI job ID format below (we include job IDs and timestamps, not raw build artifacts):
   - job_v2: 2026-09-15T17:08:21Z # job id 49822
   - job_v2: 2026-09-22T04:13:09Z # job id 50212

Internal note: Irene, Marissa — I included a short note on our ephemeral credential rotation (see my message below). Naomi, flag if you want me to include KMS audit links.

Connor O'Brien
Senior Compliance Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: sdlc-custody-ledger-20261012.csv; ci-build-ids-logs-20261013.txt; pr-approval-snapshots-20261013.pdf



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@assessco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-14T11:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SIG intake — request for SDLC custody artifacts

Adding a quick explanatory snippet about PR gating: our merge pipeline enforces an "approval-tag" requirement on protected branches. The approval tag is generated when a user with PR-approval role signs off in the reviewer UI; that event is immutably stamped into the PR metadata and exported to the custody ledger generator. Example event row (in ledger):

PR: #2437 | repo: infra/gate | approver_role: "release-owner" | approver: "Naomi F" | ts: 2026-09-15T17:07:44Z | deploy_target: prod-us-west-2

On the CI side we store job IDs and immutability hashes in the build metadata (we don't store full logs in the ledger). The attached CI log file contains the job ids and the minimal trace we use for assessor review.

Jordan Blake
Director, Reliability Engineering

source.subject: Reconcile MSA clauses + order form rate reclassification
messages:
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@novahealth.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>
Cc: NovaHealth Procurement <procurement@novahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2026 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: MSA / Order form — rate tier reclassification + exhibit alignment

Hi Dylan,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement and finance reviewed the draft order form and identified two items that need reconciliation before we can approve:

1) Exhibit B (rate ladder) appears to have an older rate block that conflicts with the MSA fee schedule (Section 2.1). We need the order form to reference the current MSA clause or explicitly indicate the overridden rates.
2) The service-level exhibit in the SOW references "Dedicated Burst" credits that NovaHealth will not use — we want those removed and reallocated to baseline throughput.

I've attached our updated forecast (NovaHealth_usage_forecast_Q4.csv) and a short markup of the order form. Can your commercial/legal team produce a consolidated redline to the MSA + order form that we can share with our counsel?

Time sensitivity: our internal procurement window closes 10/16; ideally we land final language this week or early next. If needed we can move to DocuSign once redlines are agreed.

Attaching: OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_marked.pdf (contains inline comments)

Thanks,
Priya
Priya Shah
Head of Procurement, NovaHealth
priya.shah@novahealth.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

--- Forwarded attachment stubs ---
OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_marked.pdf (pdf)
NovaHealth_usage_forecast_Q4.csv (csv)

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@novahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2026 17:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: MSA / Order form — rate tier reclassification + exhibit alignment

Hi Priya — thanks for the clear notes and the forecast. I pulled the team in (commercial + legal) and attached a consolidated redline of the Order Form and a proposed Exhibit B that aligns with the MSA language.

High level plan / next steps:
- Our commercial redline (attached) replaces the legacy rate ladder with the updated tiers and calls out the MSA clause that governs overrides.
- We removed the "Dedicated Burst" line item and proposed reallocating those units into Baseline Throughput SKU — see Exhibit B, table row three.
- Karthik (legal) will review the MSA cross-reference and confirm whether we should add an explicit "governing order of precedence" sentence or keep the existing language.

Can you confirm: do you want a single consolidated amendment (MSA + Order Form) or two documents (MSA amendment + separate Order Form rewrite)? Either works for us but procurement often prefers a single amendment for signature.

I attached: OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_redline.pdf and Exhibit-B-rate-ladder_v2.docx

Proposed sync: 10/12 at 10am PT to run through the final commercial items — I can send a calendar invite if that works.

Thanks,
Dylan
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference
--- Attachment stubs ---
OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_redline.pdf (pdf)
Exhibit-B-rate-ladder_v2.docx (docx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA / Order form — rate tier reclassification + exhibit alignment

Dylan / team — quick read-through:

- MSA precedence: current MSA Section 11.2 is a general precedence clause but it's permissive. I recommend adding a single sentence in the Order Form header: "For clarity, the pricing terms set forth in Exhibit B to this Order Form shall govern to the extent of any inconsistency with the MSA." That keeps the MSA intact but gives procurement the explicit language they asked for.
- Removing "Dedicated Burst" is commercially fine. We should add a short implementation note in the SOW so infra ops knows the SKU mapping during onboarding.
- Suggested redline: accept the Order Form exhibit as provided, add the precedence sentence, and include a 30-day post-signing implementation window for SKU reallocation.

I'll attach a one-page legal memo with the exact clause to drop in and a final tracked-change doc by EOD.

Karthik
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
--- Quoted from Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com> on 2026-10-09 17:05:00 -0400 ---
> Hi Priya — thanks for the clear notes and the forecast. I pulled the team in (commercial + legal) and attached a consolidated redline of the Order Form and a proposed Exhibit B that aligns with the MSA language.

From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@novahealth.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>
Cc: NovaHealth Procurement <procurement@novahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 13:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: MSA / Order form — rate tier reclassification + exhibit alignment

Dylan / Karthik — thanks, that approach works for us.

We prefer a single consolidated amendment that includes the Order Form and the Exhibit B table (procurement wants fewer signature pages). If you can add the precedence language and the 30-day implementation note, NovaHealth legal will do a quick review. Targeting approval internally by 10/15.

Next steps:
- Please send final tracked-change doc by EOD 10/12 (Karthik noted EOD).
- If legal is good, deliver the DocuSign envelope; we'll countersign and issue a PO.

Small ask: can we set the effective date to 10/20 so billing aligns with our finance cut? If not, note it in the amendment and we can handle proration.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this week.
Priya
--- Quoted from Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> on 2026-10-12 08:22:00 -0700 ---
> Dylan / team — quick read-through:
> 
> - MSA precedence: current MSA Section 11.2 is a general precedence clause but it's permissive. I recommend adding a single sentence in the Order Form header: "For clarity, the pricing terms set forth in Exhibit B to this Order Form shall govern to the extent of any inconsistency with the MSA." That keeps the MSA intact but gives procurement the explicit language they asked for.

Hi Priya — thanks for the clear notes and the forecast. I pulled the team in (commercial + legal) and attached a consolidated redline of the Order Form and a proposed Exhibit B that aligns with the MSA language.

High level plan / next steps:
- Our commercial redline (attached) replaces the legacy rate ladder with the updated tiers and calls out the MSA clause that governs overrides.
- We removed the \"Dedicated Burst\" line item and proposed reallocating those units into Baseline Throughput SKU — see Exhibit B, table row three.
- Karthik (legal) will review the MSA cross-reference and confirm whether we should add an explicit \"governing order of precedence\" sentence or keep the existing language.

Can you confirm: do you want a single consolidated amendment (MSA + Order Form) or two documents (MSA amendment + separate Order Form rewrite)? Either works for us but procurement often prefers a single amendment for signature.

I attached: OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_redline.pdf and Exhibit-B-rate-ladder_v2.docx

Proposed sync: 10/12 at 10am PT to run through the final commercial items — I can send a calendar invite if that works.

Thanks,
Dylan
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference
--- Attachment stubs ---
OrderForm_NovaHealth_v3_redline.pdf (pdf)
Exhibit-B-rate-ladder_v2.docx (docx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA / Order form — rate tier reclassification + exhibit alignment

Dylan / team — quick read-through:

- MSA precedence: current MSA Section 11.2 is a general precedence clause but it's permissive. I recommend adding a single sentence in the Order Form header: \"For clarity, the pricing terms set forth in Exhibit B to this Order Form shall govern to the extent of any inconsistency with the MSA.\" That keeps the MSA intact but gives procurement the explicit language they asked for.
- Removing \"Dedicated Burst\" is commercially fine. We should add a short implementation note in the SOW so infra ops knows the SKU mapping during onboarding.
- Suggested redline: accept the Order Form exhibit as provided, add the precedence sentence, and include a 30-day post-signing implementation window for SKU reallocation.

I'll attach a one-page legal memo with the exact clause to drop in and a final tracked-change doc by EOD.

Karthik
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

We prefer a single consolidated amendment that includes the Order Form and the Exhibit B table (procurement wants fewer signature pages). If you can add the precedence language and the 30-day implementation note, NovaHealth legal will do a quick review. Targeting approval internally by 10/15.

Next steps:
- Please send final tracked-change doc by EOD 10/12 (Karthik noted EOD).
- If legal is good, deliver the DocuSign envelope; we'll countersign and issue a PO.

Small ask: can we set the effective date to 10/20 so billing aligns with our finance cut? If not, note it in the amendment and we can handle proration.

Priya

source.subject: EU-only processing and deletion evidence request
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-10T09:12:00Z
Subject: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking this — Novamed's privacy & security team reviewed the materials from your team and we have a couple of follow-ups specific to our EU-only processing requirement (Schrems II / SCCs scope). Can you please confirm the following for our account:

1) Is production inference, backups, and snapshot storage kept physically within the EU region(s) (we'd prefer eu-central-1 / eu-west-1 only)?
2) Does the control plane (auth / metadata / job orchestration) store any cleartext identifiers or payload outside the EU for our account? If yes, what pseudonymization / encryption controls are applied and where are keys held?
3) Retention: what are the default retention windows for inference inputs, transient KV caches, model state snapshots, and audit logs?
4) Deletion proof: what artifacts can Redwood provide to prove data erasure (deletion manifests, snapshot IDs, audit log entries, checksums)?
5) Operator access: please describe conditions for human operator access to EU-hosted data and whether operator access is restricted to EU-based staff / controlled bastion.

We attached our security questionnaire (Novamed_Security_Questions_v2.pdf). This is time-sensitive — ideally we have a written response + evidence bundle within five business days and a short call to review.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Privacy, Novamed
elena.ruiz@novamed.com

Attachment: Novamed_Security_Questions_v2.pdf (pdf, 48KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-10T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list. I'm looping in Naomi (privacy lead) and Jordan (ops) who will put together the evidence set.

Quick plan:
- Naomi will provide a written technical response outlining residency, control plane behavior, and operator access controls.
- Jordan will prepare a sample deletion manifest and the set of audit artifacts we can produce (hashes, timestamps, snapshot IDs).
- We can share an SCCs addendum and operator affidavit for Novamed to review.
- If helpful, we can schedule a 30-minute call tomorrow or Friday to walk through the artifacts.

Can you confirm: do you want the evidence bundle packaged as a single ZIP and uploaded to a secure share, or would you prefer separate docs uploaded to DocuSign/SharePoint? Also let us know any specific format constraints for deletion proof (CSV manifest, signed JSON, etc.).

We aim to get the first pass to you within 3 business days and the full evidence bundle within 5.

Thanks,
Ava
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

---
Ava Chen
Redwood Inference

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-11T14:30:00Z
Subject: Re: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Elena — thanks for the questions. Below is a concise, account-scoped response for Novamed; I've focused on the controls and concrete artifacts we can provide. Note: these controls are enabled for Novamed's Dedicated EU-only deployment we provisioned during onboarding. If you were discussing a multi-tenant hosted plan, some control-plane metadata would follow a different path — we confirmed Dedicated EU-only is in place for your tenancy.

1) Physical residency
- Compute and persistent volumes: all inference compute, model caches, and persistent snapshots for Novamed are provisioned in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) and replicated only to eu-west-1 for cross-az redundancy; no replicas are placed outside the EU.
- Backups & snapshots: on a retention cycle configured for your tenancy (default 7 days for hourly snapshots, 90-day cyclic backup overwrite policy) — all stored in EU S3 buckets under our EU data partition.

2) Control plane behavior and keys
- Control plane: for Dedicated EU-only customers we run a private control plane within your VPC in the EU. Metadata that might include request identifiers is pseudonymized at ingestion and encrypted at rest with a customer-scoped CMK (if you provide one) or our EU-resident CMK by default. No cleartext inference payload leaves the EU.
- Key management: option to bring-your-own-key (BYOK) in an EU KMS (customer-managed) or use Redwood-managed EU CMK. Key operations for BYOK remain in EU.

3) Retention windows (defaults, can be tightened per contract)
- Inference inputs (raw request bodies): ephemeral by default, 24 hours unless flagged for debugging — configurable to 0 retention for strict accounts.
- KV / prefix cache: kept in-memory and in-region; cache eviction policies apply.
- Snapshots: hourly snapshots retained 7 days; full backups subject to your backup policy (we set 90-day overwrite by default).
- Audit logs: stored for 365 days in EU (append-only + write-once logs). We can extend retention on request.

4) Deletion proof and artifacts we can provide
- Deletion manifest (CSV) listing object paths, snapshot IDs, timestamps, and SHA256 checksums of the deleted objects.
- Audit log extracts showing erasure events with operator / automation identity and an immutable sequence number.
- Snapshot lifecycle records and storage lifecycle operations with timestamps (S3 DELETE markers, object version IDs).
- Operator affidavit + compact SCCs addendum stating transfer controls in place (see attached redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf).

Attachments we will include in the bundle: redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf, deletion_manifest_template.csv, inregion_network_diagram.png.

5) Operator access
- Human operator access for Dedicated EU-only customers is restricted by role and requires an approved JIT (just-in-time) access request. Access is logged and requires an auditable justification. For Novamed we have restricted operator access to EU-based operations staff per your contract.
- Emergency access: remote troubleshooting runs via ephemeral SSH bastions with session recording; evidence of sessions is included in the audit extracts.

Next steps:
- Jordan will prepare a sample deletion manifest and a small audit extract for the last 30 days.
- Ava will coordinate delivery and the secure upload.
- We can provide a signed operator affidavit and SCCs addendum as separate PDF attachments.

If you need any of the items signed (DocuSign) or require a prescriptive CSV layout for the deletion manifest to match your internal intake, let us know and we'll adapt.

Regards,
Naomi Feldman
Director, Privacy & Compliance, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf (pdf, 112KB); deletion_manifest_template.csv (csv, 3KB); inregion_network_diagram.png (png, 98KB)

> On Oct 10, 2026, at 09:12, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com> wrote:
>
> > Hi Ava,
> >
> > Thanks for taking this — Novamed's privacy & security team reviewed the materials...

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, privacy@novamed.com
Date: 2026-10-12T16:45:00Z
Subject: Re: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Ava / Naomi / Jordan —

This is very helpful, thank you. Novamed would like the full evidence bundle as a single signed ZIP (S/MIME or DocuSign delivery) that contains:
- Deletion manifest (CSV) covering the last 6 months for the three test tenants we ran during onboarding
- Audit extracts with SHA256 hashes and immutable sequence numbers
- Snapshot deletion records and S3 object version IDs
- Signed operator affidavit and SCCs addendum

We also attached our contract redlines referencing retention and operator residency (Novamed_contract_redlines.docx). Could you confirm an ETA for the bundle and send a DocuSign link for any items that require signature? Once we have the artifacts we will run them past our legal and privacy teams and revert with any follow-ups.

Proposed next step: 30-min call Wednesday 2026-10-14 09:00 CET to review the bundle. Please confirm availability.

Thanks,
Elena
Head of Privacy, Novamed

Attachment: Novamed_contract_redlines.docx (docx, 24KB)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@medbridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurements@medbridge.com
Date: Fri, 9 Oct 2026 15:24:00 -0700

source.subject: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse
messages:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Diane Cho <diane.cho@novapulse.com>, Alex Mercer <alex.mercer@novapulse.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-07T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Hi Diane / Alex —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. As promised, attached is a concise MAP for the GCP private-offer path we discussed. The goal here is to align milestones across Engineering, Procurement, and Finance so your deployment and invoice flow are predictable. Key items in this version:

- Staged acceptance gates (smoke test → production validation → SLA handoff) with owner and target dates
- Procurement checklist for your SOW signoff and PO to the marketplace private-offer
- Billing handoff: recommended invoice cadence and account alias to avoid double-billing in GCP

Action items for this week:
1) Procurement: confirm required legal language for marketplace POs (payment term / remittance).
2) Engineering: confirm acceptance checklist items and a target 7-day validation window post-deploy.
3) Finance: confirm preferred invoice recipient and GL code for mapping.

I attached: NovaPulse_MAP_v3.pdf (MAP), NovaPulse_SOW_lineitems.xlsx (SOW line items), and a short invoice guidance text file.

If that looks good I can push the DocuSign packet for the SOW and coordinate an acceptance window starting Oct 19.

Thanks —
Lauren Bishop
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0124

Attachments: NovaPulse_MAP_v3.pdf (pdf), NovaPulse_SOW_lineitems.xlsx (xlsx), Invoice_Instructions.txt (txt)

From: Diane Cho <diane.cho@novapulse.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@novapulse.com>, Alex Mercer <alex.mercer@novapulse.com>
Date: 2026-10-07T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Hi Lauren —

Appreciate the clear MAP and the attachments. A couple of quick clarifying questions from Procurement before we confirm dates:

1) For the marketplace private-offer, can Redwood accept a PO referencing the private-offer SKU and our GCP billing alias? Our accounting system requires the invoice to list the marketplace offer id.
2) For payment terms: we usually do Net 45. Would Redwood accept Net 45 on this deal if the invoice is issued via the marketplace listing?
3) On acceptance: can you confirm a rollback plan and who owns the last-mile validation failures (NovaPulse vs Redwood) during the 7-day validation window?

We can corp-sign quickly once those are answered — happy to sync live if easier.

Thanks,
Diane Cho
Director, Procurement | NovaPulse
Diane.Cho@novapulse.com

(Procurement attachment: procurement_requirements_vNov.pdf)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Diane Cho <diane.cho@novapulse.com>
Date: 2026-10-07T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Quick answers for Procurement items — tagging Legal/Finance in-thread for confirmation but want to keep the momentum:

1) Marketplace invoice: Yes, we can map the private-offer SKU to your GCP billing alias. We'll include the marketplace offer id and your alias on the invoice. Operational note: for a GCP private-offer the marketplace issues the invoice, but Redwood will provide the required billing metadata to ensure your AR system matches it.

2) Payment terms: We can accept Net 45 on the invoice routed through marketplace BUT we need the signed SOW to reflect the acceptance gates and the billing recipient. If Finance approves (cc'd), we can confirm in writing.

3) Acceptance ownership: Redwood will own service-level validation and remediation for any infra/runtime-related failures during the 7-day validation. NovaPulse will own any customer-data-specific integration regressions (mapping/data schema errors). We'll include a short rollback play in the acceptance appendix.

Next steps I recommend: Legal & Finance confirm Net 45 ok (cc'd), Diana/Procurement confirm PO alias and remit details, and we schedule a short 30-min close call Thursday to freeze dates.

Thanks —
Karthik Iyer
Head of Solutions | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

From: Alex Mercer <alex.mercer@novapulse.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Diane Cho <diane.cho@novapulse.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@novapulse.com>
Date: 2026-10-08T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Thanks Karthik — that alignment helps. From Engineering side a couple of practical asks:

- Can Redwood provide the acceptance smoke-test checklist as a short YAML or JSON snippet we can run in CI? That will reduce the validation window time.
- For rollback: we need a defined snapshot timestamp and a single contact to trigger a rollback. Can Jordan be our point?

Procurement is ready to issue the PO as soon as we get confirmation of the invoice metadata and the Net 45 ok. We're aiming to issue PO by Oct 12 so Oct 19 acceptance window still looks feasible.

—Alex
Alex Mercer
Engineering Manager | NovaPulse
alex.mercer@novapulse.com

On Thu, Oct 7, 2026 at 2:20 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Quick answers for Procurement items — tagging Legal/Finance in-thread for confirmation but want to keep the momentum:
> 
> 1) Marketplace invoice: Yes, we can map the private-offer SKU to your GCP billing alias. We'll include the marketplace offer id and your alias on the invoice.
> 2) Payment terms: We can accept Net 45 on the invoice routed through marketplace BUT we need the signed SOW to reflect the acceptance gates and the billing recipient.
> 3) Acceptance ownership: Redwood will own service-level validation and remediation for any infra/runtime-related failures during the 7-day validation.

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Diane Cho <diane.cho@novapulse.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@novapulse.com>, Alex Mercer <alex.mercer@novapulse.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-09T14:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Diane / team —

Great — thanks for the quick answers and momentum. Final confirmations:

- Finance has approved Net 45 for invoice issued via marketplace; we'll document this in the SOW appendix and on the marketplace invoice metadata.
- Jordan will be your rollback and escalation contact during the 7-day validation: jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com, +1 (612) 555-0199. He'll own snapshot coordination and the rollback trigger.
- Attachment: I added a short smoke-test YAML snippet to the MAP (see link) and appended a one-paragraph rollback play.

Next operational steps:
1) NovaPulse Procurement issues the PO referencing the private-offer SKU and your GCP alias by Oct 12.
2) I will send the DocuSign SOW tomorrow morning with the acceptance appendix and invoice metadata baked in.
3) Once SOW is signed we’ll request the private-offer activation from GCP marketplace; target deployment week Oct 18 and start 7-day validation on Oct 19.

If you prefer a 20-minute alignment call tomorrow we can take quick clarifying items — otherwise I’ll send DocuSign and the close checklist.

Thanks everyone — excited to get NovaPulse live.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com

Attachments referenced: NovaPulse_MAP_v3.pdf, NovaPulse_SOW_lineitems.xlsx, Invoice_Instructions.txt, smoke_test_snippet.yml (in MAP link)

> - For rollback: we need a defined snapshot timestamp and a single contact to trigger a rollback. Can Jordan be our point?

source.subject: Re: Urgent — tail latency with long-context requests (32k) impacting Fable Health
messages:
From: Ana Ruiz <ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com>
To: dylan.brooks@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 15:45:00 -0500
Subject: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Hi Dylan,

Today at ~11:30am PT our production integration started seeing huge tail latency on calls that include long patient-history prompts (~28k–32k tokens). p95 climbed from ~220ms to 3.2s and p99 to 8.7s; error rate spiked briefly to 6%. This is hitting our scheduled summarization jobs and causing timeouts in our pipeline.

We tried reducing concurrency and that helped marginally, but the behavior persists and is intermittent. I’ve attached a short sample of request IDs and timestamps below.

Sample request ids: rwd-ff8c-9a2c, rwd-ff9d-3b1e, rwd-ffa1-4c6b
Approx prompt sizes: 27,900 — 32,100 tokens

This is impacting a critical customer workflow and we need an ETA on root cause and mitigation. Can you escalate?

Thanks,
Ana
Ana Ruiz
Head of Integrations — Fable Health
ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com

Attachments: (log excerpts referenced; full debug requested)

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Team — forwarding an urgent customer report from Fable Health. They’re seeing p95/p99 regressions and intermittent errors when sending ~32k token requests. Customer impact: production summarization jobs failing/timeouts.

Immediate asks:
- Logan: please triage — can you confirm whether this is a runtime regression or capacity/config issue? Include whether this ties to the kernel changes we rolled last week (v2.3.4).
- Jordan: can you coordinate the customer comms + credit/SLA remediation if we need to offer one?
- Evelyn: requesting leadership awareness; if this looks like a systemic regression we may need exec-level outreach.

I attached the minimal log bundle Fable provided and a short timeline. I’ll stand by on a call if needed.

Attachments: redwood_debug_log_tenant_fable_20261005.tar.gz

—
Dylan Brooks
Senior Account Executive, Hosted — Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 18:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Running quick triage notes — preliminary findings below (summary first):

Summary:
- Root cause (likely): increased frequency of KV-cache misses combined with a fallback to a less-optimized kernel path when sequence length >~24k on a subset of GPU families. This increases compute and memory churn which pushes some requests into higher-latency execution paths.
- Contributing factor: a change in kernel selection logic deployed in v2.3.4 (last week) appears to prefer a new attention kernel that performs well for mid-length sequences but regresses for explicit 32k windows under certain initial KV cache states.
- Repro: repros locally with tenant prompt payloads provided by customer; p99 latency goes from ~600ms to ~8–9s when we force the same kernel fallback.

Immediate mitigations I can take now:
1) Rollback the kernel selection tweak for the hosted pool handling this tenant (risk: slight perf loss for mid-length but prevents long-tail) — ETA 2 hrs for canary + 30m to monitor.
2) Apply a temporary tenant-level throttle for requests with context > 24k (rate-limit config) to reduce concurrent long-window contention — ETA 45m.
3) Push a configuration to enable more aggressive prefix/KV caching for this tenant (reduces cache misses) — ETA 1 hr.

Attached: short trace output and the debug archive Dylan forwarded. I’ll open a SUP ticket and link the traces.

Technical notes:
- Affected nodes: hosted-gpu-pool-7 (A100v4 family), hosted-gpu-pool-3 (A100v3 subset) — memory footprint spikes observed.
- Observed error spike appears to be due to per-request OOM guard trips on the fallback path; errors recover once fallback finished.

I’ll start with a conservative rollback on a small canary ring to validate. Recommend messaging the customer that we have a mitigation plan and ETA ~3hrs for initial rollback/controls.

—
Logan Wright
SRE — Serving Runtime, Redwood Inference

Attachments: repro_traces.txt, sup_4827_link.txt (SUP-4827)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com, support@fablehealth.com
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Thanks Logan — appreciate the quick triage. Proposed action plan for customer comms & ops:

1) Immediate customer message (I drafted below) — apologize + note mitigations and ETA. Offer a proactive credit if we miss SLA windows.
2) Ops: run rollback canary ASAP and enable tenant throttle + KV cache tuning for Fable.
3) Product follow-up: investigate long-term guardrails — reject/queue extreme-size requests or route to dedicated capacity if they exceed live thresholds.

Draft message to Fable (please edit):
---
Ana — thanks for the report and the logs. We’ve identified a high-confidence mitigation path: we will rollback a kernel-selection change that is increasing tail latency for 24k–32k context lengths, and we will enable tenant-level rate limits for very large prompts while we monitor. We expect the first mitigation (canary rollback + config) within ~3 hours and will update you when the canary is validated. We are prepared to offer a service credit if this impacts your SLAs.
---

Request: Dylan — please send the message to Ana once rollback canary starts and confirm the credit offer details with Sales/Finance. Evelyn — can you sign off on the credit threshold (suggest 10% of this month’s usage for affected pipelines)?

I’ll follow with a short postmortem and product proposal for long-term handling of extreme-length prompts.

—
Jordan Blake
Product — Hosted Inference

Attachments: sla-exception-request-fable.docx

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com, ops-leads@redwood.ai, finance-partners@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 18:58:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Good work triaging — thanks Logan and Jordan. Approve the rollback canary and the temporary tenant throttle. FYI to leadership: please treat as Sev2 with customer escalation; if rollback fails or we see regression across >3 hosted pools, escalate to Sev1.

Action items I’m assigning:
- Logan: execute canary rollback within next 2 hours and post canary metrics to the Drive link (include p95/p99 and error-rate).
- Dylan: send the edited customer message within 30 minutes of canary start and include the proactive credit language. Coordinate with Finance on the credit amount (Jordan suggested 10%).
- Jordan: draft the postmortem outline and include early product guardrail items (reject/route/queuing for >24k).
- Ops: create an incident in PagerDuty and link SUP-4827.

Customer message to send (approved):
---
Ana — we’ve identified a likely cause and are deploying a rollback + tenant-level protections now. Initial mitigation will be started immediately and we expect to have the canary validated within ~3 hours. We will follow up with a full timeline and a proposed credit for impacted runs. We sincerely apologize for the disruption and will keep you updated in the thread.
---

Keep me posted on canary results. If the canary indicates the rollback does not resolve the regressions we will prepare exec outreach and broader mitigations (dedicated routing for extreme contexts).

—
Evelyn Hart
VP, Platform & Reliability — Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced in thread: redwood_debug_log_tenant_fable_20261005.tar.gz, sla-exception-request-fable.docx

I’ll start with a conservative rollback on a small canary ring to validate. Recommend messaging the customer that we have a mitigation plan and ETA ~3hrs for initial rollback/controls.

—
Logan Wright

To: dylan.brooks@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 15:45:00 -0500
Subject: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Hi Dylan,

Today at ~11:30am PT our production integration started seeing huge tail latency on calls that include long patient-history prompts (~28k–32k tokens). p95 climbed from ~220ms to 3.2s and p99 to 8.7s; error rate spiked briefly to 6%. This is hitting our scheduled summarization jobs and causing timeouts in our pipeline.

We tried reducing concurrency and that helped marginally, but the behavior persists and is intermittent. I’ve attached a short sample of request IDs and timestamps below.

Sample request ids: rwd-ff8c-9a2c, rwd-ff9d-3b1e, rwd-ffa1-4c6b
Approx prompt sizes: 27,900 — 32,100 tokens

This is impacting a critical customer workflow and we need an ETA on root cause and mitigation. Can you escalate?

Thanks,
Ana
Ana Ruiz
Head of Integrations — Fable Health
ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com

Team — forwarding an urgent customer report from Fable Health. They’re seeing p95/p99 regressions and intermittent errors when sending ~32k token requests. Customer impact: production summarization jobs failing/timeouts.

Immediate asks:
- Logan: please triage — can you confirm whether this is a runtime regression or capacity/config issue? Include whether this ties to the kernel changes we rolled last week (v2.3.4).
- Jordan: can you coordinate the customer comms + credit/SLA remediation if we need to offer one?
- Evelyn: requesting leadership awareness; if this looks like a systemic regression we may need exec-level outreach.

I attached the minimal log bundle Fable provided and a short timeline. I’ll stand by on a call if needed.

Attachments: redwood_debug_log_tenant_fable_20261005.tar.gz

Dylan Brooks
Senior Account Executive, Hosted — Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 18:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Running quick triage notes — preliminary findings below (summary first):

Summary:
- Root cause (likely): increased frequency of KV-cache misses combined with a fallback to a less-optimized kernel path when sequence length >~24k on a subset of GPU families. This increases compute and memory churn which pushes some requests into higher-latency execution paths.
- Contributing factor: a change in kernel selection logic deployed in v2.3.4 (last week) appears to prefer a new attention kernel that performs well for mid-length sequences but regresses for explicit 32k windows under certain initial KV cache states.
- Repro: repros locally with tenant prompt payloads provided by customer; p99 latency goes from ~600ms to ~8–9s when we force the same kernel fallback.

Immediate mitigations I can take now:
1) Rollback the kernel selection tweak for the hosted pool handling this tenant (risk: slight perf loss for mid-length but prevents long-tail) — ETA 2 hrs for canary + 30m to monitor.
2) Apply a temporary tenant-level throttle for requests with context > 24k (rate-limit config) to reduce concurrent long-window contention — ETA 45m.
3) Push a configuration to enable more aggressive prefix/KV caching for this tenant (reduces cache misses) — ETA 1 hr.

Attached: short trace output and the debug archive Dylan forwarded. I’ll open a SUP ticket and link the traces.

Technical notes:
- Affected nodes: hosted-gpu-pool-7 (A100v4 family), hosted-gpu-pool-3 (A100v3 subset) — memory footprint spikes observed.
- Observed error spike appears to be due to per-request OOM guard trips on the fallback path; errors recover once fallback finished.

I’ll start with a conservative rollback on a small canary ring to validate. Recommend messaging the customer that we have a mitigation plan and ETA ~3hrs for initial rollback/controls.

—
Logan Wright
SRE — Serving Runtime, Redwood Inference

Attachments: repro_traces.txt, sup_4827_link.txt (SUP-4827)
