source.subject: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation
source.messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Exec Leadership Distro <leadership@redwood.com>; Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 08:13:00 -0700
Subject: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation

Good morning all,

Quick weekly exec staff note ahead of QBR pack grooming this afternoon. I kept it tight — wins / risks / asks. Attachment: 2026Q3_exec_brief.pdf (slide deck stub).

Wins:
- Hosted API latency improvements: median p50 reduced from 68ms to 55ms after the LRU cache fix; will show microbench on slide 4.
- Dedicated contract signed with NovaHealth (pilot starts 10/20) — +$120k ARR projected in FY27.
- Model onboarding pipeline: two new quantized variants now in the catalog (lower cost, similar perf).

Risks / open items:
- Greenleaf Analytics (DEAL-8421) reported a tail-latency regression on 4k context during peak — legal and SRE are looping, but this may surface in QBR if not resolved. See Jordan's forwarded note below.
- QBR pack: our slide on unit economics needs updated token-cost delta after the last batch optimizer change (ask to Finance & Runtime).
- Canaries for private deployments are showing a slightly higher error rate (0.6% -> 1.1%) for one region; we should decide whether to call this OUTAGE or degradation for the pack.

Asks (short):
1) Evelyn — can you own the CS/Canary action and provide a short summary (1 slide) with mitigation plan by EOD?
2) Laura — need ARR impact table and the headcount ask language for the exec pack by 15:00 PT.
3) Jordan — please share the Greenleaf escalation thread and any proposed customer-facing remediation.

I'll be in the QBR grooming at 15:30; attaching the working deck for comments.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: 2026Q3_exec_brief.pdf

----

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>; Exec Leadership Distro <leadership@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation

Noted — I'll take point on the Canary slide and include the mitigation timeline. Will add a short CS impact blurb and proposed rollback trigger.

Evelyn Hart
VP, Customer Success | Redwood Inference

----

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>; Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>; Exec Leadership Distro <leadership@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation

Aditya — I can take the ARR impact numbers but need the following to finalize before 15:00 PT:
- Finalized run-rate for NovaHealth (contract start date + billing cadence) — Finance, please confirm.
- Estimated incremental hosting cost from the new batch optimizer (SRE/Runtime to confirm token-cost delta).

Also, legal has redlines back from Greenleaf procurement (attached). If procurement expects contract language changes that affect revenue recognition, we need to surface that in the pack. I attached the redlines I received from Legal for reference.

I'll seed a slide with headcount ask language; would appreciate a one-paragraph impact statement from Product on what that hire enables.

Laura Bennett
Chief of Staff to CEO | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

Attachments: greenleaf_contract_redlines.pdf

----

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>; Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>; Exec Leadership Distro <leadership@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Customer report — Greenleaf latency spikes (P1)

Looping everyone in on the customer escalation. I flagged this P1 to SRE and Infra; CS is coordinating an interim config rollback that should reduce tail latencies within the hour. Legal is already on the redlines thread.

Forwarded message:
From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@greenleaf.ai>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 12:37:42 -0400
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, support@redwood.com
Subject: Sudden spikes in query latency during peak window

Jordan,

We've been seeing large latency spikes in the 4k-context endpoints between 11:30-12:30 ET across multiple accounts. This started after the optimizer push yesterday. It's impacting our ingestion pipelines and adds significant downstream processing delay. Please advise how quickly you can provide a mitigation or rollback; SLA credits and contract terms are a concern if this persists.

Thanks,
Alex Johnson
Head of Platform Engineering | Greenleaf Analytics
alex.johnson@greenleaf.ai

--
Jordan Blake
VP, Infra & Reliability | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 206-555-0117

Action items from my side: I asked SRE to (1) revert the batch optimizer change in the greenleaf-serving pool, (2) run targeted prefix cache warmup for their tenants, and (3) produce latency histograms for the last 48 hours for the QBR appendix. I'll confirm by 16:30 PT and post a short incident note to OPS-345.

Jordan

jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 206-555-0117

I'll confirm by 16:30 PT and post a short incident note to OPS-345.

Jordan

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: legal name variation / TIN mismatch — PO on hold
messages:
From: Sophia Vargas <sophia@quirkcloud.io>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T08:32:00-07:00
Subject: New vendor setup — QuirkCloud tax ID matches?

Hi Dylan,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. Quick follow-up from our side as we're trying to get PO RDW-4921 issued so we can start the integration next week. AP has requested the W-8BEN-E we uploaded earlier but flagged a name/TIN mismatch when they ran the cross-check against the IRS/TIN database. The legal name on the W-8BEN-E is "QuirkCloud, LLC" but our automated vendor record was created under "QuirkCloud Technologies, Inc." (we used the abbreviated legal name on some invoices).

We attach the signed W-8BEN-E and a bank letter confirming the corporate name and account. Can you confirm what exact name Redwood needs on the W-8 and whether the TIN shown (12-3456789) is sufficient or if you need an EIN notarized letter?

We understand the PO may be on hold but wanted to surface this early — happy to provide any notarized materials or a signed addendum. Apologies for the confusion.

Best,
Sophia Vargas
Head of Finance, QuirkCloud
[Attachment: QuirkCloud_signed_W8BEN-E.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: QuirkCloud_bank_letter.pdf (pdf)]

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophia Vargas <sophia@quirkcloud.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T10:14:00-07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — QuirkCloud tax ID matches?

Hi Sophia,

Thanks for the quick follow-up and for sending the docs. We're seeing the exact issue AP called out: the vendor record name and the legal name on the tax form don't match closely enough for our TIN verification step. Two quick clarifying points from our side: 

1) For US-source payees, we require the legal entity name on the W-8/W-9 to match the name associated with the TIN/EIN in IRS records. Small trade names or DBA entries usually trigger a manual review.
2) If the IRS name/TIN database doesn't match, AP asks for a secondary proof of EIN: either an IRS CP 575 notice, a bank letter showing the EIN, or a notarized copy of state formation documents showing the registered name and EIN.

Next steps I recommend:
- If QuirkCloud's registered legal name is "QuirkCloud Technologies, Inc.", please re-sign a W-9 (if US) or W-8BEN-E with that exact registered name.
- If the entity is indeed an LLC with a different registered name, provide one of the accepted EIN proofs above.

I'll keep PO RDW-4921 in hold status until AP confirms receipt of acceptable evidence. Monica — can you confirm AP's preferred proof (bank letter vs CP 575) for this flow and whether we can accept the attached bank letter as sufficient for PO release? Jordan — FYI on downstream SOW start schedules if this slips.

Sophia, thanks — if it helps we can accept a notarized PDF via DocuSign and then push the PO through same day once AP signs off.

Best,
Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Vendor Onboarding | Redwood Inference
[Internal: PO RDW-4921 on hold]

From: Aaron Chu <aaron.chu@quirkcloud.io>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sophia Vargas <sophia@quirkcloud.io>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:58:00-07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — QuirkCloud tax ID matches?

Hi Dylan / Monica,

Appreciate the guidance. Quick background: QuirkCloud went through a name change last year for branding — the legal entity on file with the state is "QuirkCloud Technologies, Inc." (EIN 12-3456789). Some invoices and our AWS/Stripe accounts still show "QuirkCloud, LLC" which was their former DBA.

Attached are: 1) a bank letter on official letterhead that lists QuirkCloud Technologies, Inc. and the EIN; 2) a PDF of the state formation amendment showing the name change. Both are notarized. 

Could AP accept the bank letter + formation amendment as proof? If not, we can pull a CP 575 or get a letter from our tax counsel.

Attachments: QuirkCloud_signed_W8BEN-E.pdf, QuirkCloud_bank_letter.pdf

Thanks — Aaron
Finance Operations, QuirkCloud

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Aaron Chu <aaron.chu@quirkcloud.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: New vendor setup — legal name variation / TIN mismatch — PO on hold

Hi all,

Confirmed with AP: the notarized bank letter together with the state formation amendment is acceptable evidence to clear the TIN/name mismatch for issuing a PO. We will require the vendor to: 1) resubmit a W-8BEN-E using the registered name exactly as on the formation amendment ("QuirkCloud Technologies, Inc."); and 2) attach the notarized bank letter in the vendor profile.

Once that's uploaded, AP will run the final verification and, assuming the TIN cross-checks, release PO RDW-4921. Should be same-day after upload.

Aaron/Sophia — please upload the corrected W-8 and bank letter to the DocuSign verification link (or reply to this thread with the notarized PDFs) and I'll kick the verification.

Jordan — heads up, if PO releases today the integration kickoff can still start on 10/07 as planned; if it slips we'll rebook.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Senior Manager, Vendor Operations | Redwood Inference
[Action items: vendor re-upload corrected W-8; AP to run final TIN verification; PO RDW-4921 to be released after positive match]

source.subject: Demo invite — time showing incorrectly after forward
source.thread:
From: Tom Müller <tom.mueller@acme-eu.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya@bellwether.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 10:12:00 +02:00
Subject: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Hi Ben,

Appreciated the intro call earlier. Would love to show your team a short walkthrough of our integration next Tuesday, Oct 6 — I put a 30-minute slot at 10:00 (CEST).

Attached is the calendar invite (demo-invite.ics) and a link to the Meet: https://meet.google.com/zoom-placeholder

If that doesn't work, tell me a couple of windows and I'll adjust.

Best,
Tom
Senior Solutions Engineer
Acme EU

Attachment: demo-invite.ics (text/calendar)

-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tom Müller <tom.mueller@acme-eu.de>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 09:35:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Tom — thanks, looks great. Quick heads up: when I accepted the invite, my calendar showed the start time as 04:00 (AM) on Oct 6 instead of 10:00. I think the TZ info dropped when Priya forwarded the invite to me earlier (she originally forwarded it from her account). Can you confirm the intended timezone and whether you can resend with an explicit TZ/UTC timestamp?

I attached a screenshot — my Google Calendar appears to be treating the event as a "floating" time (no TZ attached) which is why it converted to my local 04:00.

Thanks,
Ben
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
Attachment: ical-screenshot.png (image/png)

-----
From: Priya Shah <priya@bellwether.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 11:00:00 +05:30
Subject: Fwd: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Ben — sorry, I forwarded Tom's invite from my mobile. On some devices the ICS loses the TZID header and becomes a floating event. Tom, please resend with TZID or send times in UTC if possible so Ben's calendar picks it up cleanly.

—Priya
Partner Success Manager
Bellwether.ai

-----
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 09:45:00 -04:00
Subject: RE: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Looping in Laura since we may want to propose alternate windows that work across EU/US/APAC. This is a common gotcha when partners forward mobile ICS files — Google Calendar sometimes interprets them as "floating" and applies the recipient's date but not the intended zone.

Options I recommend we give Tom (all converted to local labels for clarity):
- Tue Oct 6, 09:00–09:30 CEST (14:00–14:30 SGT / 08:00–08:30 BST) — morning EU-friendly
- Tue Oct 6, 08:00–08:30 CEST (13:00–13:30 SGT / 07:00–07:30 BST) — earlier slot
- Tue Oct 6, 15:00–15:30 CEST (20:00–20:30 SGT / 14:00–14:30 BST) — later slot to hit US East afternoon as well

Alternatively ask Tom to: 1) resend ICS with TZID (Europe/Berlin or Europe/Paris) OR 2) send a UTC start time in the event description (e.g., 08:00 UTC / 10:00 CEST). I'm attaching a corrected ICS we can send back to him showing explicit TZID (stub).

Regards,
Jordan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachment: corrected-demo-invite.ics (text/calendar)

-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tom Müller <tom.mueller@acme-eu.de>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya@bellwether.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 09:50:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Tom — thanks. I think the easiest path is one of the three slots Jordan suggested (below) or you can resend the invite with an explicit TZ. My calendar currently shows it as 04:00 (which would be too early).

Proposed slots (CEST / local equivalents):
- Option A — Oct 6, 09:00 CEST (Ben: 03:00 EDT, Jordan: 09:00 BST) — fine for teammate in EU; not ideal for US but earliest EU window
- Option B — Oct 6, 13:00 CEST (Ben: 07:00 EDT, Jordan: 13:00 BST) — middle ground
- Option C — Oct 6, 15:00 CEST (Ben: 09:00 EDT, Jordan: 15:00 BST) — later EU slot that hits US morning

If you can resend with TZID set to Europe/Berlin or include "Start: 2026-10-06T08:00:00Z" as UTC in the body that will solve the "floating" behavior. If easier, feel free to pick a slot and I will confirm immediately.

Thanks — Ben
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference

-----
From: Tom Müller <tom.mueller@acme-eu.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya@bellwether.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 17:05:00 +02:00
Subject: Re: Quick demo next week — Oct 6 at 10:00

Thanks for the heads up — my apologies, I forwarded the original from Outlook and Priya then forwarded from mobile which probably stripped the TZ. I just created a fresh invite and set the DTSTART with TZID=Europe/Berlin and also added the UTC start in the description. New event link: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcd1234

I'll keep it at Oct 6, 10:00 CEST (which should show as 04:00 EDT for Ben) but I added the UTC line and the Meet link again. If that still shows wrong on your side, please let me know and I'll send a plain-text calendar time in the body as well.

See you then,
Tom
Senior Solutions Engineer
Acme EU

Attachment: demo-invite-resend.ics (text/calendar)

-----
Notes:
- Thread resolved by resending ICS with explicit TZID and adding UTC fallback in description.
- Internal action: Jordan to update HubSpot meeting record HS-9834 to reflect confirmed time and include note about forwarded-ICS TZ issue.

source.subject: DocuVerify: POA & signer routing for Acme MSA/Order Form
source.messages:
From: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <procurement@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — ready to sign

Hi Alex / Naomi,

Thanks — Acme is ready to proceed with signature on the MSA and Order Form.
We have a temporary POA on file for Samir Patel (VP Finance) so he will execute on behalf of the company for this transaction. I can attach the declaration and a scanned letter from the corporate secretary.

Requested routing: DocuSign to Samir (procurement@acme.com) as primary signer, then to Elena for acknowledgement (elena.ross@acme.com). Please include the Order Form (OrderForm_12345.pdf) as an attachment and the clean MSA we received back from your team.

Do you require any additional evidence of authority? Happy to provide whatever you need.

Best,
Elena Ross
Senior Counsel, Acme Corporation
elena.ross@acme.com

---

On Mon, 27 Sep 2026 at 09:25 AM Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com> wrote:
> To: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>
> Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
> Date: Mon, Sep 27, 2026 at 9:25 AM
> Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — ready to sign
>
> Thanks, Elena — good to hear.
>
> Quick asks from our side before we route:
> 1) Please attach the POA declaration and the corporate secretary letter (PDF). We need a signed doc that explicitly authorizes Samir to sign commercial contracts of this size.
> 2) Confirm the exact email address you want DocuSign routed to (procurement@acme.com as in your note?).
> 3) Tell us whether Acme requires any witness or countersignature fields in DocuSign (some customers need a witness line for POA signings).
>
> Karthik / Jordan — tagging you to review the POA formatting once Elena sends it. If legal OKs, Jordan can create the envelope and include the audit-acceptance checkbox and the "signing on behalf of" declarative text.
>
> We plan to send a single envelope for MSA + Order Form; Redwood will sign last after we receive countersigned copies.
>
> Alex Martinez
> Sr. Account Lead, Redwood Inference
> alex@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
>
>
> On Mon, 27 Sep 2026 at 09:40 AM Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com> wrote:
> > Alex — attaching the POA (Acme_POA_declaration.pdf) and the sec-letter.
> > Samir's primary routing email is procurement@acme.com. There is no external witness required; the corporate secretary letter includes a notarization stamp.
> >
> > I see that Redwood wants Samir to sign and then Redwood to countersign. That works. We prefer the envelope to show the signer name as: "Samir Patel (on behalf of Acme Corporation)" and we will ensure the signer's identity is verified on our end.
> >
> > Attaching: Acme_POA_declaration.pdf, SecLetter_Acme_signed.pdf
> >
> > Elena Ross
> > Senior Counsel, Acme Corporation
> > elena.ross@acme.com
>
>
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POA docs — internal review

All — I reviewed the POA declaration Elena sent. High level: the POA contains the delegation language we need and is notarized. A couple of notes for the envelope: 
- Add a short declarative checkbox under Samir's signature: "I am signing on behalf of Acme Corporation under delegated authority."
- Enable the DocuSign audit trail and retain for 7 years per our retention policy (please set storage flag).
- Add an optional signature field for Elena if Acme wants counsel to acknowledge (not required).

If Jordan can build the envelope with those items, legal is good to proceed. I've attached a quick contract-acceptance checklist (legal_checklist_POA.txt).

Karthik Iyer
Head of Legal Ops, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

---

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>, Samir Patel <procurement@acme.com>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: DocuSign routing and final clean copy

Hi Elena / Samir —

source.subject: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization
messages:
From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

Team — attaching this week’s deal-weighted scorecard and the pipeline snapshot as of EOD 2026-09-27. Key ask: we need a quick prioritization on which opportunities to seed with reserved capacity versus burst credits for the next 30–60 day runway.

High-level notes (see attachment for per-deal numbers):
- NovaApps (DEAL-87654): high-touch pilot + PoV. Sales requests 24 reserved A100-equivalent slots for 6 weeks starting Nov 1. Pipeline indicates potential MC close in Q1.
- Orion.ai and Helio Labs are lower-touch; recommend burst-only unless they top the commit list.
- Risk/mitigation: we currently have 72 reserved slots unassigned across east/us and west/us pools.

Asks by EOD today:
1) Jordan — confirm sales priority order for these top 6 deals and whether NovaApps is top 1.
2) Karthik — confirm feasibility of a 24-slot reservation in west/us for Nov 1 and any infra frictions (images, quantization variants).
3) Laura — please flag any financial guardrails if we move 24 slots to a pilot reserve (impact to committed spend and how quickly we can reallocate).

Attachments: deal_weighted_scorecard_Q4.xlsx (spreadsheet), pipeline_snapshot_20260927.csv (csv)

Thanks,
Chloe Martin
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

On Tue, Sep 28, 2026 at 3:12 PM Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — attaching this week’s deal-weighted scorecard and the pipeline snapshot as of EOD 2026-09-27. Key ask: we need a quick prioritization on which opportunities to seed with reserved capacity versus burst credits for the next 30–60 day runway.
>
> Asks by EOD today:
> 1) Jordan — confirm sales priority order for these top 6 deals and whether NovaApps is top 1.

Chloe — NovaApps is top priority for Q4 outreach and pilot support. Sam’s team is sensitive to performance jitter; they’ve asked for reserved capacity to meet latency SLOs during the pilot.

Priority I’d propose (top 4 to consider for reserves):
1. NovaApps (DEAL-87654) — yes, top 1
2. Helio Labs (DEAL-88112) — medium reserve if capacity permits
3. Orion.ai (DEAL-87221) — burst preferred
4. AtlasEdge (DEAL-86302) — low priority for reserve

If infra can commit the 24 slots for NovaApps on Nov 1, I’ll push procurement to sign a 6-week pilot MSA and convert to a commit if usage hits thresholds.

-Jordan
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

Chloe / team — quick infrastructure feasibility note.

- We have an unassigned west/us physical pool of 40 A100-equivalent slots and east/us pool of 32. After scheduled maintenance and the expected burst window for next week (internal experiments), realistic available reserveable capacity for Nov 1 is ~48 slots across regions.
- Reserving 24 slots for NovaApps in west/us is feasible but:
  * We need provisioning of the customer image and quantized model variant by Oct 15 to validate start-of-runway perf.
  * If NovaApps requires pre-warming (long KV cache lifetimes) that reduces burst headroom — we’ll need to set a strict autoscale/fallback policy.
- Recommendation: approve 24-slot reserve in west/us, but mark it as convertible reserve: if NovaApps converts to a committed annual, we’ll shift to a reserved contract; otherwise, release after 6 weeks.

Action items for infra (me):
- Reserve 24 west/us slots tentatively; hold until Laura signs off on budget movement.
- Create a tiny runbook for pre-warming and fallback to burst; link to OPS ticket when created.

Attachment: pipeline_snapshot_20260927.csv (used for region mapping)

Karthik Iyer
Head of Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>, procurement@novaapps.com
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 17:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

Thanks all — quick readout and next steps: 

Decision for now:
- Approve a tentative 24-slot west/us reserve for NovaApps starting Nov 1, contingent on finance sign-off by Oct 8 and infra validation of image/quantization by Oct 15.

Action owners and deadlines:
- Laura (Finance): confirm budget movement and any required PO/process by Oct 8.
- Karthik (Infra): create OPS ticket and runbook for pre-warm/fallback; confirm slot reservation window by Oct 15.
- Jordan (Sales/AE): kick off procurement conversation with NovaApps and share target MSA terms; coordinate pilot start dates.
- Marcus (Product): advise if any model variants require special kernel selection; loop in runtime team if needed.

I’ll consolidate into a short mutual action plan doc and post the HubSpot action (87654). If finance greenlights, Karthik can mark the slots in the reserve ledger and we’ll schedule a 30-minute handoff call next Wed (Oct 13) if needed.

Links: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/OPS-2213 | HubSpot: 87654-NovaApps

Attachments: deal_weighted_scorecard_Q4.xlsx

Best,
Chloe Martin
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

- Approve a tentative 24-slot west/us reserve for NovaApps starting Nov 1, contingent on finance sign-off by Oct 8 and infra validation of image/quantization by Oct 15.

Action owners and deadlines:
- Laura (Finance): confirm budget movement and any required PO/process by Oct 8.
- Karthik (Infra): create OPS ticket and runbook for pre-warm/fallback; confirm slot reservation window by Oct 15.
- Jordan (Sales/AE): kick off procurement conversation with NovaApps and share target MSA terms; coordinate pilot start dates.
- Marcus (Product): advise if any model variants require special kernel selection; loop in runtime team if needed.

I’ll consolidate into a short mutual action plan doc and post the HubSpot action (87654). If finance greenlights, Karthik can mark the slots in the reserve ledger and we’ll schedule a 30-minute handoff call next Wed (Oct 13) if needed.

Links: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/OPS-2213 | HubSpot: 87654-NovaApps

Attachments: deal_weighted_scorecard_Q4.xlsx

Best,
Chloe Martin
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: deal_weighted_scorecard_Q4.xlsx (spreadsheet), pipeline_snapshot_20260927.csv (csv)

Thanks,
Chloe Martin

source.subject: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi Marta,

Thanks again for the time today. Per our discussion I wanted to capture the open questions we need to close before we finalize the private-offer economics for Acadia Health (ACD-2026-4721):

1) Rebate slab definition — you mentioned a 12–24 month commit with tiered volume rebates. Can you confirm which usage buckets you expect to count toward the tier (monthly consumption vs committed reserved hours)?

2) Channel passback — your procurement team needs a clear invoice-level passback path to the reseller (they called this "distributor visibility"). We typically provide a monthly reconciliation extract plus a mapping doc showing how the marketplace settlement flows map back to reseller POs. I can attach an example workbook — will that satisfy finance?

3) Evidence for audit — please list exactly which artifacts procurement will require for the passback (invoice PDFs, marketplace settlement CSV, signed reseller contract). We want to avoid surprises in the approval cycle.

4) Implementation timing — if we include a carveout for certain SKUs (e.g., reserved capacity), do you need a contract amendment or is a marketplace private-offer note sufficient?

I'll prepare a short example calculation (illustrative numbers) and a one-page flow diagram that maps settlement → reseller → Acadia. Attaching those and looping in Jordan (channel ops) and Naomi (finance) so we can align on the specific artifacts.

Next step: if you can reply with the list of artifacts and the preferred bucket definition by EOD Friday (9/11), we will produce a reconciled workbook for your procurement and run it by the AWS marketplace rep for confirmation.

Thanks,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan_park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0186

Attachment: (planned) acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (xlsx) — sample calculation; marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (pdf) — settlement map
From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 09 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:

- Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.

- Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.

- Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.

Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.

Best,

Marta Ruiz
Director, Procurement — Acadia Health
marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com | +1 212-555-0102
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Marta — thanks, noted.

Attaching the requested artifacts: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (worked example with net-of-reseller-commission rows) and marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (one-page flow diagram mapping the settlement csv to invoice lines). High-level notes below and a short worked example inside the workbook.

Summary of the sample calculation (row references from the workbook):
- Committed hours: 10,000 node-hours/month at list price $0.20/hr → gross list $2,000/month.
- Reseller commission: 12% of list → $240 retained by reseller.
- Rebate slab applied at 10% net-of-commission → rebate $176 (10% of net $1,760).
- Net to customer after rebate: $2,000 - $240 - $176 = $1,584/month.

We include a reconciliation tab showing how the AWS settlement CSV maps to the invoice line — marketplace SKU → private-offer SKU → invoice line id. That mapping is what HealthResell needs to show the passback on their reseller invoice.

Next steps: 1) If procurement agrees to the net-of-reseller treatment, Naomi and I will draft the amendment language. 2) We'll engage the AWS marketplace rep for a short confirmation on settlement timing and the specific settlement field names used for mapping. I'm + Jordan on scheduling a 30-min call with HealthResell next week.

Cheers,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachments: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx, marketplace_passback_flow.pdf

On Wed, Sep 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Ethan,
> 
> Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:
> 
> - Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.
> 
> - Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.
> 
> - Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.
> 
> Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.
> 
> Best,
> 
> Marta
From: Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi all — looping in some AWS marketplace detail to make sure the mapping is clean for settlement. A couple of operational points: 

1) Settlement cadence: AWS typically posts settlements on a monthly cadence with a settlement CSV that contains SKU-level charges, charges-basis, and the partner/fiscal identifier. The CSV includes both list and the marketplace discount fields; you should be able to match the partner_fiscal_id to HealthResell for reconciliation.

2) Invoice mapping: Private offer line items can include a field that points back to the private-offer ID. If you populate that on the invoice line, the settlement CSV will contain the private-offer reference for easier mapping.

3) Evidence: For audit-friendly mapping send (a) the settlement CSV, (b) the reseller invoice PDF, and (c) the private-offer definition (json/pdf) that shows which SKUs and pricing applied. If you want, I can run a short extract of last month's settlement for the private-offer test account — send me the private-offer ID.

Happy to join a call if useful.

Regards,
Jacob Lin
AWS Marketplace — Distribution Partnerships
aws-marketplace@amazon.com
From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Ethan / Jacob — thanks for the clear examples and the AWS confirmation. Procurement has reviewed the workbook and confirms the net-of-reseller treatment is acceptable so long as HealthResell is named in the amendment and the monthly invoice includes a line that can be reconciled to the settlement CSV.

Next steps from Acadia:
- Please prepare a short amendment (1 page) that: names HealthResell as the reseller, states rebate slabs net-of-reseller-commission, and specifies the settlement artifacts required monthly.
- Please share the private-offer ID (so Jacob can extract the last full settlement) and we'll route a DocuSign to our legal team.

If you can send the amendment draft and the private-offer ID by EOD Thursday (9/23), we will target signature the following week.

Marta

On Thu, Sep 10, 2026 at 8:25 AM Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marta — thanks, noted.
> 
> Attaching the requested artifacts: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (worked example with net-of-reseller-commission rows) and marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (one-page flow diagram mapping the settlement csv to invoice lines). High-level notes below and a short worked example inside the workbook.
> 
> Summary of the sample calculation (row references from the workbook):
> - Committed hours: 10,000 node-hours/month at list price $0.20/hr → gross list $2,000/month.
> - Reseller commission: 12% of list → $240 retained by reseller.
> - Rebate slab applied at 10% net-of-commission → rebate $176 (10% of net $1,760).
> - Net to customer after rebate: $2,000 - $240 - $176 = $1,584/month.
> 
> We include a reconciliation tab showing how the AWS settlement CSV maps to the invoice line — marketplace SKU → private-offer SKU → invoice line id. That mapping is what HealthResell needs to show the passback on their reseller invoice.
> 
> Next steps: 1) If procurement agrees to the net-of-reseller treatment, Naomi and I will draft the amendment language. 2) We'll engage the AWS marketplace rep for a short confirmation on settlement timing and the specific settlement field names used for mapping. I'm + Jordan on scheduling a 30-min call with HealthResell next week.
> 
> Cheers,
> 
> Ethan Park
>

4) Implementation timing — if we include a carveout for certain SKUs (e.g., reserved capacity), do you need a contract amendment or is a marketplace private-offer note sufficient?

I'll prepare a short example calculation (illustrative numbers) and a one-page flow diagram that maps settlement → reseller → Acadia. Attaching those and looping in Jordan (channel ops) and Naomi (finance) so we can align on the specific artifacts.

Next step: if you can reply with the list of artifacts and the preferred bucket definition by EOD Friday (9/11), we will produce a reconciled workbook for your procurement and run it by the AWS marketplace rep for confirmation.

Thanks,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan_park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0186

Attachment: (planned) acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (xlsx) — sample calculation; marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (pdf) — settlement map


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 09 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:

- Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.

- Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.

- Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.

Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.

Best,

Marta Ruiz
Director, Procurement — Acadia Health
marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com | +1 212-555-0102


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Marta — thanks, noted.

Attaching the requested artifacts: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (worked example with net-of-reseller-commission rows) and marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (one-page flow diagram mapping the settlement csv to invoice lines). High-level notes below and a short worked example inside the workbook.

Summary of the sample calculation (row references from the workbook):
- Committed hours: 10,000 node-hours/month at list price $0.20/hr → gross list $2,000/month.
- Reseller commission: 12% of list → $240 retained by reseller.
- Rebate slab applied at 10% net-of-commission → rebate $176 (10% of net $1,760).
- Net to customer after rebate: $2,000 - $240 - $176 = $1,584/month.

We include a reconciliation tab showing how the AWS settlement CSV maps to the invoice line — marketplace SKU → private-offer SKU → invoice line id. That mapping is what HealthResell needs to show the passback on their reseller invoice.

Next steps: 1) If procurement agrees to the net-of-reseller treatment, Naomi and I will draft the amendment language. 2) We'll engage the AWS marketplace rep for a short confirmation on settlement timing and the specific settlement field names used for mapping. I'm + Jordan on scheduling a 30-min call with HealthResell next week.

Cheers,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachments: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx, marketplace_passback_flow.pdf

On Wed, Sep 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Ethan,
> 
> Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:
> 
> - Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.
> 
> - Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.
> 
> - Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.
> 
> Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.
> 
> Best,
> 
> Marta


From: Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi all — looping in some AWS marketplace detail to make sure the mapping is clean for settlement. A couple of operational points: 

1) Settlement cadence: AWS typically posts settlements on a monthly cadence with a settlement CSV that contains SKU-level charges, charges-basis, and the partner/fiscal identifier. The CSV includes both list and the marketplace discount fields; you should be able to match the partner_fiscal_id to HealthResell for reconciliation.

2) Invoice mapping: Private offer line items can include a field that points back to the private-offer ID. If you populate that on the invoice line, the settlement CSV will contain the private-offer reference for easier mapping.

3) Evidence: For audit-friendly mapping send (a) the settlement CSV, (b) the reseller invoice PDF, and (c) the private-offer definition (json/pdf) that shows which SKUs and pricing applied. If you want, I can run a short extract of last month's settlement for the private-offer test account — send me the private-offer ID.

Happy to join a call if useful.

Regards,
Jacob Lin
AWS Marketplace — Distribution Partnerships
aws-marketplace@amazon.com


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Ethan / Jacob — thanks for the clear examples and the AWS confirmation. Procurement has reviewed the workbook and confirms the net-of-reseller treatment is acceptable so long as HealthResell is named in the amendment and the monthly invoice includes a line that can be reconciled to the settlement CSV.

Next steps from Acadia:
- Please prepare a short amendment (1 page) that: names HealthResell as the reseller, states rebate slabs net-of-reseller-commission, and specifies the settlement artifacts required monthly.
- Please share the private-offer ID (so Jacob can extract the last full settlement) and we'll route a DocuSign to our legal team.

If you can send the amendment draft and the private-offer ID by EOD Thursday (9/23), we will target signature the following week.

Marta

source.subject: Post-collection allocation: mapping partial wires to invoices and VAT treatment
messages:
From: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: URGENT: Two incoming wires — need help allocating to open invoices and VAT guidance

Hi Ben / Laura,

Apologies for the mid-week drop — ACME Finance pushed two separate wires that we need your help allocating. Details below: 

- Wire A (2026-09-15, 14:03 CET): €1,150. Reference: ACME/RED/0915
- Wire B (2026-09-16, 08:47 CET): €820. Reference: ACME/RED/0916

Open invoices in our ledger: 
- INV-1001: €1,200 (goods) + VAT 19%
- INV-1002: €450 (services) + VAT 19%
- INV-1003: €320 (consulting) VAT-exempt (reverse charge expected)

Total outstanding (invoices): €1,970 + VAT adjustments. The two wires total €1,970 exactly, but VAT treatment is mixed and our remittance file only includes wire references — no invoice numbers. Can you confirm how Redwood will apply these receipts and whether VAT should be reclaimed or booked as recoverable?

Attaching the bank remittance notice: ACME_wire_notice_0916.pdf

We'd like allocation and VAT tagging confirmed by Monday EOB (20 Sep) to close our cash application.

Thanks,
Lucia Romero
ACME Finance — Payments Team
accounts@acmefin.com

---
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 11:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: URGENT: Two incoming wires — need help allocating to open invoices and VAT guidance

Hi Lucia — thanks for the heads up and for sending the remittance. I pulled the bank references and sketched a first-pass allocation below based on amounts and typical ordering: 

Proposed allocation (draft):
- Wire A (€1,150) -> INV-1001 (apply €1,150 of €1,200, outstanding €50)
- Wire B (€820)  -> INV-1002 (€450) + remainder €370 -> apply €370 to INV-1003 (which is VAT-exempt)

That maps the totals to €1,970 and leaves INV-1001 with €50 unpaid. However the VAT split needs confirmation before posting: INV-1001/1002 carry 19% VAT, INV-1003 is reverse-charge/exempt. If Redwood posts by applying payments to gross invoice amounts, VAT will be implicitly matched. If we need to book VAT recoverable or create a partial credit note, finance should advise. 

I've attached a quick CSV with the draft allocation: acme_allocation_draft_20260917.csv (headers: invoice, invoice_amount, paid_amount, vat_code). Could Laura confirm the VAT ledger code and whether to create a credit for the €50 residual or hold it on receivable? Also tagging request: include bank trace id ACME/RED/0916 on all postings.

If you prefer, I can schedule a 15-min call to walk through.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0192

Attachments: acme_allocation_draft_20260917.csv

---
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 08:30:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: Proposed allocation for ACME GmbH — finance input needed

Team — forwarding Lucia's message and my draft allocation. Key points for finance ops: 

Quoted message:
> From: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
> Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 09:12:00 +0000
> Subject: URGENT: Two incoming wires — need help allocating to open invoices and VAT guidance
> 
> (see original for details)

My draft allocation (again):
- Wire A €1,150 -> INV-1001 (partial, €50 short)
- Wire B €820  -> INV-1002 (€450) + INV-1003 (€370)

Questions for Laura: 
1) Ledger VAT code: ACME is a German entity — apply VAT-GER-19 to invoices 1001/1002? For INV-1003 should we record reverse-charge code VAT-REV-GER or mark tax-exempt?
2) Residual €50: create a short-payment memo and leave as AR open, or issue a small debit/credit to settle? If ACME requests an automatic offset (they generally prefer credit notes), we should agree wording for the credit note memo.
3) Attachments/traces: I've attached the customer remittance and bank trace we received (bank_trace_0916.pdf) — please ensure the bank reference ACME/RED/0916 migrates to the cash application record.

Can we commit to a firm posting plan by EOD Monday (20 Sep)? If finance confirms VAT reclaim vs credit note, I'll compile the final allocation and send to ACME.

Thanks — Ben

Attachments: bank_trace_0916.pdf

---
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 15:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-collection allocation: mapping partial wires to invoices and VAT treatment

Hi Lucia / Ben,

Thanks for the clear notes — finance has reviewed the draft allocation and confirmed the posting approach. Final allocation below (now posted):

- INV-1001 (€1,200 + VAT 19%) — applied €1,150 from Wire A. Outstanding balance: €50. We will leave a short-payment AR open for €50 and include note "partial payment — awaiting customer confirmation". VAT will be matched to the portion paid. VAT ledger code: VAT-GER-19.
- INV-1002 (€450 + VAT 19%) — fully paid from Wire B (€450 portion). VAT ledger code: VAT-GER-19.
- INV-1003 (€320, reverse-charge) — applied remaining €370 from Wire B; because INV-1003 is reverse-charge, we recorded the receipt against principal and will not claim VAT on the item (tax code VAT-REV-GER). The €50 residual across invoices is not subject to VAT reclaim at this time.

Actions performed: 
- Cash application posted 2026-09-21 with bank ref ACME/RED/0916.
- Generated credit note NOT required for now — we will hold the €50 on AR pending ACME confirmation. If ACME confirms that €50 is intended for fee reversal, we will issue a credit note. 
- Uploaded posting proof and allocation CSV to our record. See attachments: ACME_allocation_final_20260921.csv and redwood_allocation_note_20260921.pdf

Lucia, please confirm if ACME would like us to apply the €50 to the next invoice or have Redwood issue a credit note. If we do not hear back by close of business Friday (24 Sep), we'll leave it open per above.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Accounting Manager — Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0123

Attachments: ACME_allocation_final_20260921.csv, redwood_allocation_note_20260921.pdf

---

- INV-1002 (€450 + VAT 19%) — fully paid from Wire B (€450 portion). VAT ledger code: VAT-GER-19.
- INV-1003 (€320, reverse-charge) — applied remaining €370 from Wire B; because INV-1003 is reverse-charge, we recorded the receipt against principal and will not claim VAT on the item (tax code VAT-REV-GER). The €50 residual across invoices is not subject to VAT reclaim at this time.

Actions performed: 
- Cash application posted 2026-09-21 with bank ref ACME/RED/0916.
- Generated credit note NOT required for now — we will hold the €50 on AR pending ACME confirmation. If ACME confirms that €50 is intended for fee reversal, we will issue a credit note. 
- Uploaded posting proof and allocation CSV to our record. See attachments: ACME_allocation_final_20260921.csv and redwood_allocation_note_20260921.pdf

Lucia, please confirm if ACME would like us to apply the €50 to the next invoice or have Redwood issue a credit note. If we do not hear back by close of business Friday (24 Sep), we'll leave it open per above.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Accounting Manager — Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0123

Attachments: ACME_allocation_final_20260921.csv, redwood_allocation_note_20260921.pdf

---

source.subject: Top-up concession & co-term pathway with MediSage
messages:
From: Sasha Li <sasha.li@medisage.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: MediSage Procurement <procurement@medisage.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mid-contract top-up + co-term question

Hi David —

Quick note from our side: usage spiked in August and we need an additional 0.5M token/month capacity for the next 9 months. Procurement asked whether we can: (1) top-up mid-contract, (2) align the new capacity to our Jan 1 co-term, and (3) get a short-term discount given the incremental scale.

Can you send options (pricing and billing) and any procurement language we should expect? We’re on the existing Annual-2025 contract and would prefer minimal legal churn.

Thanks — Sasha
Sasha Li
Head of ML Platforms, MediSage
--
MediSage | sasha.li@medisage.com
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Sasha Li <sasha.li@medisage.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mid-contract top-up + co-term question

Hi Sasha —

Thanks for the heads up. We can do a mid-contract top-up with two practical paths:

Option A — Add-on top-up (fast, minimal legal change)
- Immediate add-on: +0.5M tokens/month for 9 months
- Rate: $0.012/token (10% off published add-on rate)
- Billing: invoiced monthly, non-refundable usage bucket
- No contract amendment; we’ll add an addendum to the existing order.

Option B — Co-term annualize (preferred for predictability)
- We annualize the top-up into a Jan 1 co-term: bill pro-rated amount now, then annual invoice on Jan 1
- Effective blended rate: $0.0105/token (20% off published, reflects annual commit)
- Requires simple amendment (1 page) to join the new Jan 1 term.

We can also include a short-term usage voucher (one-time credit) to soften the immediate billing impact if you want a bridge. Example: $10k voucher applied to November invoice.

I’m attaching your Q2 usage report and a draft SOW language for the addendum. If procurement wants the co-term, we’ll route legal the single-page amendment; if not, we can do the add-on today.

Attached: MediSage_SOW_v2.pdf; usage_report_Q2.csv

If you’d like, I can loop Jordan (renewals) and Naomi (pricing approvals) to confirm the discounts — otherwise, approve a path and I’ll send paperwork.

Best,
David
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0192
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Mid-contract top-up + co-term question — approval request

Jordan / Naomi —

Forwarding Sasha’s request from MediSage. TL;DR: +0.5M tokens/month for 9 months; customer prefers co-term to Jan 1 but will accept add-on if legal needs to move faster. Recommended commercial: co-term annualize (Option B) at $0.0105/token blended, with a $10k one-time voucher to offset near-term invoices.

Why this ask: keeps the customer on a predictable Jan renewal cadence and locks incremental consumption into a commit that lowers unit cost vs ad-hoc add-on.

Questions for pricing/legal:
- Approve the 20% blended discount and the $10k voucher?
- Approve one-page amendment template (I attached current draft SOW and usage report).

If you prefer, we can do a 12% discount and skip voucher (lower $ impact to revenue). Need confirmation so I can reply to Sasha and route to legal/finance.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Sasha Li <sasha.li@medisage.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mid-contract top-up + co-term question
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: MediSage Procurement <procurement@medisage.com>

[original message quoted above]

Attachments: MediSage_SOW_v2.pdf, usage_report_Q2.csv

Thanks,
David
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Sasha Li <sasha.li@medisage.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Mid-contract top-up + co-term question — approval request

Thanks for the context, David —

We’ll approve Option B (co-term annualize) at the blended rate you proposed: $0.0105/token. Approve the $10k voucher as a one-time concession tied to signing the amendment by Oct 1 (helps lock timing). Please document the voucher as a line-item credit on the initial invoice.

Action items for you:
- Send the single-page amendment to Sasha for signature (I’ll get legal to fast-track review).
- Create a DocuSign packet with the amendment + SOW and include the voucher line on the first invoice.
- Finance: route revenue recognition notes internally after signature.

Sasha — happy to move forward on this path. David will share the DocuSign link within 24 hours.

Jordan Blake
Head of Renewals, Redwood Inference

On Thu, Sep 16, 2026 at 08:25 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Jordan / Naomi —
> 
> Forwarding Sasha’s request from MediSage. TL;DR: +0.5M tokens/month for 9 months; customer prefers co-term to Jan 1 but will accept add-on if legal needs to move faster. Recommended commercial: co-term annualize (Option B) at $0.0105/token blended, with a $10k one-time voucher to offset near-term invoices.
> 
> [trimmed quoted attachments]

--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Request to pause SummitWorks demo — LLM effort reprioritized
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@summitworks.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SummitWorks demo — Wed Sep 14 confirmation & agenda

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the time last week. Per our conversation, I booked a 45-minute demo for Wednesday, Sep 14 at 11:00am PT to walk through Redwood Inference for your ML team (low-latency serving, cost controls, and private deployment options).

Proposed agenda:
- 5m intros and goals for the session
- 15m demo: hosted vs dedicated vs private deployments
- 10m Q&A on security/compliance and integration with your infra
- 15m next steps / mutual action plan

If that slot still works, we’ll circulate a calendar invite with a short pre-demo checklist (team, sample payloads). If not, tell me two alternative windows next week and we’ll rebook.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachment: redwood-demo-deck.pdf (slide stubs)
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@summitworks.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 15:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SummitWorks demo — Wed Sep 14 confirmation & agenda

Aditya —

Really appreciate you setting this up. Unfortunately, I need to push back on the demo for now. Our leadership just completed a roadmap review and has pulled the LLM pilot out of the Q3 sprint — they want the team to focus on a metrics/observability push for the next six weeks.

Given that, can we pause the demo and hold off on new calendar invites? I attached a short excerpt from our Q3 roadmap so you can see the decision context. We still want to evaluate Redwood later; please check back in Jan (after our Q4 planning) to see if we can re-prioritize.

Sorry for the late change — travel + exec bandwidth are the driver. Thank you for understanding.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Product, SummitWorks
elena.morales@summitworks.com

Attachment: SummitWorks_Q3_Roadmap.pdf
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: SummitWorks demo — paused by prospect (roadmap shift)

Mina / Jordan — forwarding Elena's note. SummitWorks just deprioritized the LLM pilot in favor of an observability push; they asked us to pause demos and suggested re-contact in Jan.

Immediate asks: 
- Mina: can we flag HS-8754123 for a nurture cadence (quarterly check-ins) and set a reminder for first week of Jan 2027?
- Jordan: any promotional/care-credit we should offer to keep the relationship warm (trial credits, product update invite)?

I'm proposing a short nurture plan: 1) send a one-page recap of value props tailored to their upcoming metrics work, 2) invite the product team to a webinar on low-cost inference patterns in November, 3) touch base in Jan with any new performance/price improvements.

Forwarded message (original below)

On Fri, 11 Sep 2026 at 15:27 Elena Morales <elena.morales@summitworks.com> wrote:
> Aditya —
> 
> Really appreciate you setting this up. Unfortunately, I need to push back on the demo for now. Our leadership just completed a roadmap review and has pulled the LLM pilot out of the Q3 sprint — they want the team to focus on a metrics/observability push for the next six weeks.
> 
> Given that, can we pause the demo and hold off on new calendar invites? I attached a short excerpt from our Q3 roadmap so you can see the decision context. We still want to evaluate Redwood later; please check back in Jan (after our Q4 planning) to see if we can re-prioritize.
> 
> Sorry for the late change — travel + exec bandwidth are the driver. Thank you for understanding.
> 
> Best,
> Elena
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@summitworks.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SummitWorks demo — Wed Sep 14 confirmation & agenda

Elena —

Thanks for the update and the roadmap excerpt — that context helps. Totally understand the reprioritization; travel and exec bandwidth often reshuffle launch plans. We’ll cancel the demo invite and pause any outreach until you give us the green light.

If helpful, we can:
- Share a one-page brief focused on cost/latency tradeoffs that maps to observability use cases (I’ll send this next week),
- Invite your product team to a short, topic-specific webinar (Nov 10) we’re running on inference cost optimization, or
- Put a gentle check-in reminder on our side for the first week of Jan 2027.

Would you prefer we hold off entirely and only re-open the thread if you reach out, or would you like a single check-in email in early Jan?

Either way, appreciate the transparency — happy to support when the project surfaces again.

Best regards,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachment: redwood-nurture-playbook.pdf (internal nurture assets)

- Put a gentle check-in reminder on our side for the first week of Jan 2027.

Either way, appreciate the transparency — happy to support when the project surfaces again.

Best regards,
Aditya Rao

aditya@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: invoice channel selection and envelope scenarios
messages:
From: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
To: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the onboarding call earlier. We want to finalize how to send invoices to Redwood once our account is activated. Two quick questions so we can update our AP playbook: 

1) Can you confirm whether Redwood prefers invoices via the vendor portal (SFTP/portal upload) or an email fallback? If email is acceptable, is there a required subject token or header we should include?

2) We often send a single email with multiple PDFs (one per PO) and also sometimes a CSV envelope mapping when there are many line items across POs. Attached is an anonymized sample CSV to show our typical envelope. Can your team confirm required column names for mapping PO -> invoice -> line items?

Attachments: lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (CSV), lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (PDF)

Appreciate a short checklist we can follow.

Best,
Finance Team
Lumos Supply
finance@lumos-supply.com


From: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
To: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
Cc: monica.patel@redwood.ai, vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Hi Lumos team — thanks for the clear samples. A few notes so we can align on the onboarding path: 

Summary recommendation (short): portal-first; email as a fallback for edge cases and PO-less invoices. 

Checklist (short):
- Portal preferred: upload single-invoice PDF per file; system auto-maps PO if PO# present in header or CSV mapping.
- Email fallback: subject token MUST contain: "Inv-Redwood:<vendor-id>" (we will provide vendor-id on activation).
- CSV envelope: required columns we need: vendor_id, invoice_number, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), total_amount (USD), po_number (optional), line_item_sku, line_item_qty, line_item_unit_price. If PO-less, put po_number blank and set invoice_type=credit_or_debit accordingly.

I attached the onboarding form you sent, plus a one-page example we use for multi-PO envelopes. Can you confirm Lumos can populate the "invoice_type" and "vendor_id" columns? If not, we can accept a wrapper file naming pattern: lumos_<vendor-id>_envelope_YYYYMMDD.csv in the subject.

Attachments: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (returned with small edits), lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (annotated)

Thanks — cc'ing Monica (onboarding) and Vanessa (billing ops) to pick up mapping details and SLA.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Manager, Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.ai

----
On Sat, Sep 12, 2026 at 09:12 AM finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP) wrote:
> Thanks for the onboarding call earlier. We want to finalize how to send invoices to Redwood once our account is activated...

From: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
To: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
Cc: sara.jenkins@acme-hr.com
Date: Sun, 13 Sep 2026 08:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Hi Aisha (and team),

Thanks — we can do portal-first. A couple of clarifications from our side before we lock the format: 

- PO-less invoices: occasionally we invoice for non-PO services (e.g., host fees). We typically leave PO empty and include a "Billing Description" column. Will validation reject rows without PO#?
- Credit notes: do you want them sent as negative-line CSVs or separate PDF credit memos with a reference?
- Vendor ID: we do not have a Redwood vendor-id field in our ERP yet. We can include the wrapper filename pattern you suggested.

Attached: lumos_answers_2026-09-13.docx (answers & sample CSV generated by our ERP).

Quick ask: if we go email fallback, what is the SLA for acknowledgements? We ask because our treasury needs a confirmation for payment cycles.

Best,
Clara Munro
AP Lead, Lumos Supply
finance@lumos-supply.com

From: jordan.blake@redwood.ai
To: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai, monica.patel@redwood.ai, vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai
Cc: finance@lumos-supply.com
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Quick reply with procurement constraints and mapping guidance.

Team — from a procurement and payment ops perspective: portal-first is required for standard PO-matched invoices. Email fallback is acceptable for: vendor go-live exceptions, PO-less invoices, or when vendors cannot connect to the portal. We should make the fallback as deterministic as possible:

1) Subject token: exact match prefix must be "Inv-Redwood:" followed by vendor-id (we will assign and share). Examples: "Inv-Redwood: LUM-5532". Subject token simplifies auto-routing.

2) Envelope CSV rules (when sending multiple invoices in one email): we will treat the CSV as the source of truth for mapping. Minimal required columns: vendor_id, invoice_number, invoice_date, total_amount, po_number (can be blank), invoice_type (INVOICE|CREDIT|DEBIT), file_reference (filename of attached PDF). If file_reference missing, system will attempt to match by invoice_number.

3) PO-less rows: allow blank po_number but require invoice_type=INVOICE and a billing_description. Flag these for manual review — payment terms default to 60 days until a finance exception is approved.

4) Acknowledgements: for email fallback we will send automated ACK within 2 business hours confirming ingestion (or explicit failure reasons). Portal uploads have immediate UI acknowledgements.

I attached a tiny example CSV snippet inline here so Lumos can replicate formatting: 

---lumos_example.csv---
vendor_id,invoice_number,invoice_date,total_amount,po_number,invoice_type,file_reference,billing_description
LUM-5532,INV-2026-0912-01,2026-09-12,12500.00,PO-8892,INVOICE,INV-2026-0912-01.pdf,Monthly hosting fees

---

If Lumos cannot populate vendor_id yet, they must use the filename wrapper pattern and include the subject token.

Jordan Blake
Sr. Procurement Lead
Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai

From: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
To: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
Cc: monica.patel@redwood.ai, vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai, jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 16:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Clara — thanks for the quick answers and for the attached doc. Next steps and decisions we agreed on: 

- Onboard path: portal-first. Lumos will pilot portal uploads for standard PO-matched invoices starting next Tuesday (2026-09-21).
- Email fallback: Lumos will use subject token "Inv-Redwood: LUM-5532" for the pilot and the wrapper filename pattern lumos_LUM-5532_envelope_YYYYMMDD.csv until vendor_id is in their ERP.
- CSV envelope: Lumos will include the columns Jordan listed; Lumos team confirmed they can add invoice_type and file_reference.
- PO-less handling: will be allowed but set to pending review with 60-day payment terms unless finance exception is approved.
- ACK SLA: Redwood will send ingestion ACKs within 2 business hours for email fallback.

Action items: 
- Lumos (Clara): share a signed onboarding form. We have sent a DocuSign link for the vendor onboarding (see Drive link).
- Redwood (Monica): configure routing rule for subject token and add Lumos to the portal pilot group.

Thanks all — pleased we have a clean path.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Manager, Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.ai

Attachments: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (signed copy expected), lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (finalized fields)

----
On Mon, Sep 14, 2026 at 10:17 AM jordan.blake@redwood.ai wrote:
> Quick reply with procurement constraints and mapping guidance...

jordan.blake@redwood.ai



From: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
To: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
Cc: monica.patel@redwood.ai, vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai, jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 16:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Clara — thanks for the quick answers and for the attached doc. Next steps and decisions we agreed on: 

- Onboard path: portal-first. Lumos will pilot portal uploads for standard PO-matched invoices starting next Tuesday (2026-09-21).
- Email fallback: Lumos will use subject token "Inv-Redwood: LUM-5532" for the pilot and the wrapper filename pattern lumos_LUM-5532_envelope_YYYYMMDD.csv until vendor_id is in their ERP.
- CSV envelope: Lumos will include the columns Jordan listed; Lumos team confirmed they can add invoice_type and file_reference.
- PO-less handling: will be allowed but set to pending review with 60-day payment terms unless finance exception is approved.
- ACK SLA: Redwood will send ingestion ACKs within 2 business hours for email fallback.

Action items: 
- Lumos (Clara): share a signed onboarding form. We have sent a DocuSign link for the vendor onboarding (see Drive link).
- Redwood (Monica): configure routing rule for subject token and add Lumos to the portal pilot group.

Thanks all — pleased we have a clean path.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Manager, Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.ai

Attachments: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (signed copy expected), lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (finalized fields)

Can you confirm the arrivals below and whether you want a single consolidated shuttle or split transfers? We want to avoid gate hold chaos.

Attendees we’re tracking: Samir (PM), Elena (sponsor), plus three engineers arriving later.

Flights I have:
- Samir Rao — AA 241, arrives SFO 12:45 PM (on time)
- Northbridge VP (Elena) — UA 980, arrives SFO 1:55 PM (tight but doable)
- Engineer block — multiple legs, some arriving 10pm (red eye)

Questions / proposals:
1) If Elena’s flight delays >30m we’ll swap to the 3pm coach pickup so Samir can cover initial check-in.

3) Seat transfer/standby policy: if an attendee can take an earlier connection, we’ll reassign seats and update manifest. Dev — can you prepare a live manifest CSV we can update?
