To: Tom Leiter <tom.leiter@bluebay.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Aug 2026 15:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BCP/DR evidence request — RTO/RPO and runbook artifacts

Tom —

Following up with the technical details you asked for. See the attached artifacts and the Drive folder linked below (we have a read-only collection for security reviews). Highlights:

- Hosted (regional failover tests): observed RTO (time from failover start to end-to-end inference validation): 95th pct = 5m 20s, 99th pct = 8m 45s (across three scheduled drills in us-west-2/us-east-1/eu-west-1; timestamps are in the attached runbook drill-log.csv with request IDs redacted).
- Dedicated: measured 95th pct RTO = 4m 10s, 99th pct = 6m 5s (note: dedicated pools have reserved capacity and automated priming).
- Private/on-prem: Redwood provides an optional warm-start agent that can be deployed by customers; for managed Private installs (contracted), Redwood can operate the warm-starts as part of an ops addendum. Operational responsibility is listed in the runbook_index_v2.xlsx under section 2.4.
- Warm-cache evidence: attached kv_warm_cache_screenshots.png (cache hit-rate graphs during a planned failover) and cloudtrail_sample.log (anonymized).

Drive (read-only): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD_example_runbook
DocuSign (statement): https://app.docusign.com/BB-4352/evidence-statement

If you want a redacted CSV of the drill log in a specific schema, tell us the fields and we’ll produce it; otherwise the attached CSV contains timestamps, stage markers, and operator notes.

Regards,

Diego Fernandez
Director, Reliability Engineering | Redwood Inference
diego.fernandez@redwood.com

Attachments: kv_warm_cache_screenshots.png, cloudtrail_sample.log, runbook_drill-log.csv

source.subject: MSA — Liability cap and damages exclusions
messages:
From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Camila —

Thanks for sending the MSA. Our legal team reviewed the draft and attached a redline (ACME_MSA_Redline.pdf). Two high-level items we need to resolve before procurement can move forward:

1) Liability cap: Our counsel prefers either a fixed cap of $5,000,000 or language that preserves carve-outs (IP indemnity, gross negligence, willful misconduct, and data breach) as unlimited. They also asked whether Redwood is open to an aggregate (total life of the agreement) cap vs. an annualized cap (per 12-month period).

2) Consequential damages: We see Redwood's standard language excludes consequential damages; counsel asked to confirm this covers indirect loss but not data-related direct remediation costs.

I've highlighted the relevant sections in the attached PDF. Happy to hop on a short call if that's easier — Sarah (cc'd) is ACME's procurement lead and will handle the commercial checklist once we have legal alignment.

Regards,
Olivia Grant
Senior Counsel, ACME Ops
olivia.grant@acmeops.com
+1 415 555 0142

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T12:35:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Olivia —

Thanks for the redline and the clear summary. Appreciate you flagging the two items. High level from our side: Redwood's negotiation posture is to keep a commercially reasonable cap tied to fees paid to provide predictability for both parties; we typically propose the greater of $1,000,000 or 2x fees paid in the prior 12 months. That structure balances customer protection while keeping liability exposure aligned with usage.

On carve-outs, we are open to preserving IP indemnity and willful misconduct as exceptions to the cap. For data breaches we usually handle confidentiality/breach through indemnity and insurance obligations rather than an unlimited cap — happy to provide policy details.

I've attached a short counter-redline (Redwood_Counter_Redline.pdf) that implements the 2x/1M floor approach and preserves the standard carve-outs: IP indemnity, gross negligence/willful misconduct, and confidentiality/data processing obligations.

Would you be able to confirm whether ACME needs an aggregate lifetime cap or an annualized cap? If you prefer aggregate, we may need to discuss a higher multiple so the economics remain balanced.

If you like, I can loop in our Legal lead (Laura Bennett) and Jordan on the commercial side to finalize.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
+1 650 555 0191

Attachment: Redwood_Counter_Redline.pdf

From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T16:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Camila —

Thanks. I reviewed Redwood's counter. A few points from our counsel:

• $1M floor + 2x prior 12 months feels low for a mission-critical vendor; they pushed for either $5M or 3x annual fees.
• For security/data incidents, counsel wants those outside the cap (they view remediation and notification costs as direct damages).
• They also asked for an exception for IP indemnity and for liability arising from Redwood's storage/processing of ACME data.

Procurement (Sarah) also prefers an aggregate cap (life of the agreement) to reduce edge cases around timing of incidents. If Redwood is willing to move to 3x annual fees with a $2M floor for aggregate caps, that may be workable — but counsel will still seek carve-outs for data breach remediation.

Can you confirm Redwood's absolute position on data breach carve-outs and whether a 3x multiple with a $2M floor is something you can present to your leadership?

-Olivia

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-04T09:06:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Laura/Jordan —

Forwarding ACME's most recent comments. They pushed back on 2x/$1M and prefer aggregate caps; counsel is asking for carve-outs for data breach remediation and wants either $5M fixed or 3x annual fees with a $2M floor.

Key asks for legal:
- Can we accept 3x prior 12 months with a $2M floor if the cap is aggregate?
- Are we able to carve out data breach remediation costs (notification, forensics, credit monitoring) from the cap, or do we limit the carve-out to willful misconduct and IP?
- Any internal policy or insurance constraints we should flag (cyber limits, reinsurance)?

Jordan — if legal is OK with the compromise, would appreciate your quick read on whether Sales can accept $2M floor / 3x multiple for this account at ACME's current ARR commitment. Deal: ACME-3451.

Thanks — Camila

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
Date: Mon, Aug 3, 2026 at 4:08 PM
Subject: Re: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>

> Camila —
> 
> Thanks. I reviewed Redwood's counter. A few points from our counsel:
> 
> • $1M floor + 2x prior 12 months feels low for a mission-critical vendor; they pushed for either $5M or 3x annual fees.
> • For security/data incidents, counsel wants those outside the cap (they view remediation and notification costs as direct damages).
> • They also asked for an exception for IP indemnity and for liability arising from Redwood's storage/processing of ACME data.
> 
> Procurement (Sarah) also prefers an aggregate cap (life of the agreement) to reduce edge cases around timing of incidents. If Redwood is willing to move to 3x annual fees with a $2M floor for aggregate caps, that may be workable — but counsel will still seek carve-outs for data breach remediation.
> 
> Can you confirm Redwood's absolute position on data breach carve-outs and whether a 3x multiple with a $2M floor is something you can present to your leadership?
> 
> -Olivia

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-04T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Camila/Jordan —

Thanks for the heads up and the context. Quick read: the customer's asks are reasonable commercially but there are tradeoffs. My recommended compromise and rationale below — this is what I would present back to ACME as a single-line counter to resolve quickly.

Recommended contract language (MSA, Section 12):
"Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Supplier's aggregate liability to Customer for any and all claims arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the greater of (a) Two and a Half (2.5) times the aggregate fees paid by Customer to Supplier in the 12 months preceding the claim, or (b) Two Million U.S. Dollars (USD $2,000,000). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the cap shall not apply to (i) claims for infringement of third-party intellectual property rights for which Supplier is required to indemnify Customer, (ii) liability resulting from Supplier's gross negligence or willful misconduct, and (iii) liabilities arising out of Supplier's intentional or unlawful retention, disclosure or misuse of Customer Data. For clarity, direct costs reasonably necessary to respond to a verified data security incident (including forensic investigation, regulatory notification, and credit monitoring where required by applicable law) shall be treated as direct damages but shall be subject to the cap defined above unless otherwise required by applicable law."

Why this compromise:
- 2.5x gives a middle ground between 2x and 3x; it reduces upside exposure for Redwood while addressing ACME's concern for a higher multiple.
- $2M floor is materially higher than $1M and helps procurement justify the deal.
- We preserve IP and willful misconduct carve-outs. For data incidents, we propose that remediation costs are "direct damages" but remain within the cap — this balances ACME's need to cover remediation while keeping unlimited exposure off the table. If ACME insists on unlimited remediation costs carve-out, we should request confirmation of minimum cyber insurance limits and consider a pricing uplift.

Internal policy/insurance: our cyber primary limit is $10M; we can provide a certificate. If ACME wants remediation costs excluded from the cap entirely, need to escalate for underwriting and pricing (potentially a 5-10% uplift depending on account size).

If you want, I can draft a redline with the above language and a short negotiating note for Jordan to use when speaking with procurement. If Sales confirms they can accept the floor/multiple, I'll prepare the redline and we can route for signature.

-Laura Bennett
General Counsel, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachment: none

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-05T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Olivia —

Thanks for the detailed feedback and for outlining counsel's priorities. We discussed internally and would like to propose the following compromise to resolve the liability cap quickly:

Proposed compromise (summary):
• Cap: greater of (i) 2.5x aggregate fees paid in the prior 12 months, or (ii) $2,000,000 USD (aggregate cap for the life of the agreement).
• Carve-outs: IP indemnity and liability for gross negligence/willful misconduct remain outside the cap.
• Data incidents: direct remediation costs (forensic investigation, regulatory notification and required credit monitoring) will be treated as direct damages; however, they will remain subject to the aggregate cap above. If ACME requires unlimited remediation costs outside the cap, Redwood will need to escalate to underwriting and pricing and request evidence of minimum cyber insurance limits.

We believe this language balances ACME's need for meaningful protection and Redwood's need for predictable exposure. Laura has drafted the clause and is happy to include it as a redline if you want to proceed.

Next steps: please confirm (a) whether ACME will accept an aggregate cap or prefers annualized, and (b) whether the proposed 2.5x / $2M compromise is acceptable. If yes, we can circulate a final redline and DocuSign link.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
+1 650 555 0191

Attachments referenced: ACME_MSA_Redline.pdf, Redwood_Counter_Redline.pdf, final-cap-summary.docx

If Redwood is willing to move to 3x annual fees with a $2M floor for aggregate caps, that may be workable — but counsel will still seek carve-outs for data breach remediation.
> 
> Can you confirm Redwood's absolute position on data breach carve-outs and whether a 3x multiple with a $2M floor is something you can present to your leadership?
> 
> -Olivia

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-04T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Camila/Jordan —

Thanks for the heads up and the context. Quick read: the customer's asks are reasonable commercially but there are tradeoffs. My recommended compromise and rationale below — this is what I would present back to ACME as a single-line counter to resolve quickly.

Recommended contract language (MSA, Section 12):
"Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Supplier's aggregate liability to Customer for any and all claims arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the greater of (a) Two and a Half (2.5) times the aggregate fees paid by Customer to Supplier in the 12 months preceding the claim, or (b) Two Million U.S. Dollars (USD $2,000,000). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the cap shall not apply to (i) claims for infringement of third-party intellectual property rights for which Supplier is required to indemnify Customer, (ii) liability resulting from Supplier's gross negligence or willful misconduct, and (iii) liabilities arising out of Supplier's intentional or unlawful retention, disclosure or misuse of Customer Data. For clarity, direct costs reasonably necessary to respond to a verified data security incident (including forensic investigation, regulatory notification, and credit monitoring where required by applicable law) shall be treated as direct damages but shall be subject to the cap defined above unless otherwise required by applicable law."

Why this compromise:
- 2.5x gives a middle ground between 2x and 3x; it reduces upside exposure for Redwood while addressing ACME's concern for a higher multiple.
- $2M floor is materially higher than $1M and helps procurement justify the deal.
- We preserve IP and willful misconduct carve-outs. For data incidents, we propose that remediation costs are "direct damages" but remain within the cap — this balances ACME's need to cover remediation while keeping unlimited exposure off the table. If ACME insists on unlimited remediation costs carve-out, we should request confirmation of minimum cyber insurance limits and consider a pricing uplift.

Internal policy/insurance: our cyber primary limit is $10M; we can provide a certificate. If ACME wants remediation costs excluded from the cap entirely, need to escalate for underwriting and pricing (potentially a 5-10% uplift depending on account size).

If you want, I can draft a redline with the above language and a short negotiating note for Jordan to use when speaking with procurement. If Sales confirms they can accept the floor/multiple, I'll prepare the redline and we can route for signature.

-Laura Bennett
General Counsel, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachment: none

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@acmeops.com>
Cc: Sarah Li <sarah.li@acmeops.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-05T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MSA - liability cap: proposal and questions

Olivia —

Thanks for the detailed feedback and for outlining counsel's priorities. We discussed internally and would like to propose the following compromise to resolve the liability cap quickly:

Proposed compromise (summary):
• Cap: greater of (i) 2.5x aggregate fees paid in the prior 12 months, or (ii) $2,000,000 USD (aggregate cap for the life of the agreement).
• Carve-outs: IP indemnity and liability for gross negligence/willful misconduct remain outside the cap.
• Data incidents: direct remediation costs (forensic investigation, regulatory notification and required credit monitoring) will be treated as direct damages; however, they will remain subject to the aggregate cap above. If ACME requires unlimited remediation costs outside the cap, Redwood will need to escalate to underwriting and pricing and request evidence of minimum cyber insurance limits.

We believe this language balances ACME's need for meaningful protection and Redwood's need for predictable exposure. Laura has drafted the clause and is happy to include it as a redline if you want to proceed.

Next steps: please confirm (a) whether ACME will accept an aggregate cap or prefers annualized, and (b) whether the proposed 2.5x / $2M compromise is acceptable. If yes, we can circulate a final redline and DocuSign link.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
+1 650 555 0191

Attachments referenced: ACME_MSA_Redline.pdf, Redwood_Counter_Redline.pdf, final-cap-summary.docx

messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive

Hi Priya —

Thanks again for the time on the call last week. The team asked for a focused architecture deep-dive to cover VPC/on-prem connectivity, air-gap constraints, and our update/patching model so your security and infra teams can validate operational fit.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue Jul 28, 10:00–11:30
- Wed Jul 29, 08:00–09:30
- Thu Jul 30, 13:00–14:30

Please let me know which works and who from your side should be on the call (security, network, procurement).

We'll have Soojin (solutions engineer) and Marissa (platform engineer) join. I attached a short checklist and our standard network diagram to give your team context. If there are any constraints (no internet egress, HSM-only KMS, private key custody), flag those and we’ll tailor the agenda.

Attachments: redwood_private_deploy_checklist.pdf, acme_network_diagram_v1.png

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 14:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive

Hi Camila —

Thanks for the proposal. We want to include our lead security engineer (Liam) and procurement (Alice) so both compliance and commercial folks can see the scope. Our team is particularly keen on: air-gapped update flow, KMS/HSM options, audit log retention, and egress controls.

From our side these slots work best (PT):
- Tue Jul 28, 10:00–11:30 (preferred)
- Thu Jul 30, 13:00–14:30 (backup)

If you can confirm attendees and share a lightweight agenda that would be great. Also — can you confirm whether 3rd-party monitoring requires outbound access, and whether you support reconnect-only update windows for air-gapped installs?

Thanks,
Priya Shah
Head of Security Operations | Acme Corp
priya.shah@acme-corp.com

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Acme: Private deployment architecture deep-dive — internal prep

Team —

Forwarding Acme's reply. They prefer Tue Jul 28 at 10:00 PT. Can you confirm who is available to cover: VPC peering/topology, HSM/KMS options, air-gap update processes, and audit/log retention?

Key asks from Acme:
- Confirm whether we can operate with no outbound egress except for managed update windows
- Detail for key management: customer KMS vs Redwood-managed HSM options
- Network topology sketches for VPC-to-on-prem and firewall rules
- What logs/audit evidence we can provide for SOC2-style review (S3 retention windows, syslog exports)

If someone can draft a 45–60 minute agenda and attach any pre-read (network diagram + checklist), I'll send the calendar invite and include procurement.

Thanks —
Camila

From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@acme-corp.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acme-corp.com>, Alice Romero <alice.romero@acme-corp.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 11:30:00 -0700

From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@zengen.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Lydia Park <lydia.park@zengen.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Quick ask — can we move the demo to align with Mark's SF trip?

Hi Marissa,

Quick note — Mark (CTO) will be in San Francisco the week of July 27–29 and really wants to join our demo in person if possible. Right now the scheduled demo is Friday the 24th at 11:00 PT, and he has a conflict that day.

Would your team be able to shift to any slot on Tuesday 28th or Wednesday 29th? Mark prefers mornings (9–11am PT) but can be flexible within those two days. Lydia (my EA) is cc'd to help coordinate logistics.

We'd like to make it in-person at our SF office — Mark's presence makes this a high priority for us. If moving is difficult, we can also do a hybrid (in-person + remote) but he prefers joining live if possible.

Thanks — appreciate the flexibility.

Best,
Ethan Ross
Senior Product Manager, ZenGen
ethan.ross@zengen.com
+1 (415) 555-0118
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@zengen.com>
Cc: Lydia Park <lydia.park@zengen.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 16:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick ask — can we move the demo to align with Mark's SF trip?

Hi Ethan —

Thanks for the heads up, and please tell Mark we're glad to make this work. I've attached a short agenda and a proposed set of time options (attached as an .ics).

Proposed windows we can cover:
- Tues 28 July — 09:00–10:00 PT or 10:30–11:30 PT
- Wed 29 July — 09:30–10:30 PT

If any of those work, I can lock it in and put together an on-site logistics note (building access, AV, headcount). Rishi will lead the product walkthrough and Connor will cover infra/latency-specific questions.

Can you confirm Mark's preferred slot and estimated on-site headcount? Also let me know if you need us to bring an NDA or additional materials.

Agenda attached: redwood-demo-agenda.pdf
ICS with proposed slots: proposed-time-options.ics

Best,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: ZenGen demo — CTO joining in person (coordination)

Folks —

ZenGen's CTO Mark Chen will likely attend in person on Tue 28th or Wed 29th. Ethan asked for mornings; I've offered 28th 09:00 or 10:30, and 29th 09:30. Rishi/Connor, please confirm which slot you prefer and whether we want to tweak the agenda to prioritize infra and cost/latency material since Mark will be there.

Logistics I need to confirm:
- In-person room + AV (I'll ask their EA for headcount)
- Visitor badges and entry instructions (we'll send room-access-instructions.txt)
- Whether to record a shorter follow-up deep-dive for their engineering leads

Jordan — can you backstop if we need to escalate AV/ops? Rishi — any additional prep or a light-run two days prior?

Thanks — planning to lock once Ethan confirms the exact hour.

—Marissa
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: ZenGen demo — CTO joining in person (coordination)

On Mon, Jul 20, 2026 at 9:12 AM Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com> wrote:
> Folks —
>
> ZenGen's CTO Mark Chen will likely attend in person on Tue 28th or Wed 29th.

Thanks — I can do Tue 28th at 10:30–11:30 PT and focus the first 25 minutes on performance & latency trade-offs, then demo the caching/quantization settings that are relevant to their cost targets. Connor, can you have a brief infra slide ready (3–5 mins) and be available for questions about KV cache sizing?

Jordan — if AV is flaky we may need a quick backup laptop + HDMI dongle. I can do a 30-min run-through on Mon 27th afternoon if that helps.

Rishi Malhotra
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
rishi@redwood.com
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@zengen.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Lydia Park <lydia.park@zengen.com>, Mark Chen <mark.chen@zengen.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick ask — can we move the demo to align with Mark's SF trip?

Marissa —

Confirmed: Mark is good to join Tue 28th at 10:30 PT in person. Headcount will be Mark + Mark's EA Lydia + one engineering lead (on-site). Lydia will help with visitor badges; she asked if you can send the building entry instructions and the exact room name.

Also, Mark asked if you could include the short performance section you've outlined up front — appreciated. Looking forward to it.

Thanks,
Ethan

--
Ethan Ross
Senior Product Manager, ZenGen
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@zengen.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Lydia Park <lydia.park@zengen.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick ask — can we move the demo to align with Mark's SF trip?

Perfect — I've reserved Conference Room A (12th floor) for Tue 28th, 10:15–11:45 PT to give us a buffer. Room instructions and visitor badge steps attached (room-access-instructions.txt).

Rishi — we'll do the 25-minute perf primer at 10:30 and then the full demo. Connor — please bring the backup laptop and the infra slide.

source.subject: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@northview.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-18T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Thanks again for the call earlier, Elena — quick recap and proposed next steps so we can move to a short, measurable eval.

Recap (what you emphasized)
- Primary goal: improve legal-doc search recall while lowering per-query cost (especially for high-concurrency retrieval).
- Key metrics you care about: recall@1/5/10, end-to-end p95 latency, and cost per 1M queries.
- Current infra: in-house vector index on GCS + on-prem FAISS for heavy queries; considering cloud-managed vector endpoints from hyperscalers.

What we propose (short matrix you can approve quickly)
1) Retrieval-only baseline (no re-rank): your embeddings + HNSW/IVF variants — measure recall@k and raw vector latency.
2) Embedding dims test: 256 / 512 / 1024 to see recall vs tokenization/size tradeoffs.
3) Rerank pipeline: retrieve topK=100 then rerank (cross-encoder) to top10 — measure added latency and recall delta.
4) End-to-end: measure client-to-result p50/p95 tail latencies for both Redwood-hosted endpoint and your current cloud provider endpoint (we'll use identical input batch).

Concrete asks from NorthView for step 1 (what we need to run):
- A sample of ~300 representative queries + expected ideal doc ids (can be partial/first-pass). Attach sample-queries.csv for format.
- A 10k-document subset (exportable) or a small example index so we can run internal microbenchmarks locally.
- Any hard cost constraint: e.g., target <$X per 1M queries (helps us pick quantization / batch).

What Redwood will deliver (48–72h micro-PoC):
- A short benchmark report with recall@1/5/10, raw retrieval latency, rerank delta, and estimated cost per 1M queries for both Redwood and one hyperscaler API (we'll target the one you mentioned on the call).
- Configuration matrix (embedding dim, index type, batch size, quantization suggestions) and recommended rollout for production.

Attachments: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf (benchmark plan) and sample-queries.csv (CSV with query,id).

Next steps / timeline
- If you can share the sample CSV + index subset by end of week we can deliver the short report early next week.
- If helpful, we can schedule a 30m slot for Jordan to walk through the benchmarking plan and answer infra questions.

Thanks — happy to iterate on the test matrix if you want a different emphasis.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
(attachment: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf; sample-queries.csv)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@northview.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Bose <samir@acme-search.com>
Date: 2026-07-18T17:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Ben — thanks for the clear summary. Quick clarifications before we hand over data: 

- The 10k subset: we can export a 10k subset of our corpus (redacted) by EOD Friday. Good.
- Sample queries: we'll provide ~300 queries; Samir will help format the expected-id column.
- Cost target: we don't have a hard cap yet, but as a rough bar please assume we want under $200 per 1M queries (at current throughput). If Redwood can show a path to 50% savings at similar recall, that's compelling.

One technical question: for rerank, when you say "cross-encoder" are you proposing a hosted model on Redwood or sending to a hyperscaler endpoint? We want both numbers: your hosted rerank (Redwood-managed) and the baseline cloud-managed rerank (to compare latency/cost).

I'll send the CSV and the subset by Friday and add Samir to any follow-ups. Thanks — happy to schedule the 30m with Jordan.

—Elena
Head of Search, NorthView Analytics

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@northview.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Samir Bose <samir@acme-search.com>
Date: 2026-07-18T18:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Elena — perfect, thanks. Short answers and additions: 

- Cross-encoder rerank: we'll run two rerank configurations in the micro-PoC: (A) Redwood-hosted cross-encoder (quantized where appropriate) and (B) a callout to the cloud provider endpoint you currently use, with identical inputs, to measure delta.

- Test matrix (what we'll run automatically):
  * Embedding dims: 256 / 512 / 1024
  * Index types: HNSW (default params), IVF+PQ (to show cost/size reductions)
  * Retrieval topK: 50 / 100 / 250 (we'll report recall@1/5/10 for each)
  * Rerank: cross-encoder on top100 -> top10; measure addtl latency.
  * Batch sizes for throughput tests: 1, 8, 32; measure p50/p95/p99 token/response latency.

- Key metrics we'll produce: recall@1/5/10, end-to-end p50/p95, per-query cost (estimate) for 1M queries, GPU/CPU utilization during tests.

- On cost targets: we'll optimize for the <$200 per 1M goal; if we can get ~40–60% savings using batching + quantized embeddings on Redwood while matching recall, we'll highlight that.

I attached the short benchmark plan (northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf) that maps test names to scripts. Once you drop the CSV + index subset I'll schedule the run and share a dedicated Drive folder with results and a short report.

Would 11am PT Tuesday for a 30m review with Jordan work?

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
(attachment: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Internal note — forwarding the thread from Elena. Key asks from NorthView: sample CSV + 10k subset by Friday and a desire to see Redwood-hosted rerank vs their cloud endpoint.

Immediate asks for the infra team (Jordan):
- Can you run a micro-benchmark next week that mirrors the plan? Target: embedding dims 256/512/1024, HNSW vs IVF+PQ, retrieve top100 then rerank to top10. Use a 10k doc subset we control; we'll replace with NorthView data when they share it.
- Outputs I need: recall@1/5/10, end-to-end p95 latency, and a back-of-the-envelope per-1M-query cost estimate for each config.

Monica — please sync with Solutions Eng to confirm we can run an apples-to-apples cloud endpoint comparison (we'll need to reproduce their request pattern and include their current provider in the run). Also flag any SOC/NDAs we need before they share the subset.

Jordan — can you confirm availability for a short run Tues morning (11am PT) and whether you need a small box or can run on Dedicated dev cluster? If Dedicated dev cluster is required, I can request a 48h reservation.

I'll capture results in HubSpot and share the Drive folder once the run starts.

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

(Forwarded attachments: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf; sample-queries.csv)

Subject: Re: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Elena — perfect, thanks. Short answers and additions: 

- Cross-encoder rerank: we'll run two rerank configurations in the micro-PoC: (A) Redwood-hosted cross-encoder (quantized where appropriate) and (B) a callout to the cloud provider endpoint you currently use, with identical inputs, to measure delta.

- Test matrix (what we'll run automatically):
  * Embedding dims: 256 / 512 / 1024
  * Index types: HNSW (default params), IVF+PQ (to show cost/size reductions)
  * Retrieval topK: 50 / 100 / 250 (we'll report recall@1/5/10 for each)
  * Rerank: cross-encoder on top100 -> top10; measure addtl latency.
  * Batch sizes for throughput tests: 1, 8, 32; measure p50/p95/p99 token/response latency.

- Key metrics we'll produce: recall@1/5/10, end-to-end p50/p95, per-query cost (estimate) for 1M queries, GPU/CPU utilization during tests.

- On cost targets: we'll optimize for the <$200 per 1M goal; if we can get ~40–60% savings using batching + quantized embeddings on Redwood while matching recall, we'll highlight that.

I attached the short benchmark plan (northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf) that maps test names to scripts. Once you drop the CSV + index subset I'll schedule the run and share a dedicated Drive folder with results and a short report.

Would 11am PT Tuesday for a 30m review with Jordan work?

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
(attachment: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-19T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-call: NorthView evaluation — cloud APIs vs Redwood (embeddings + rerank tests)

Internal note — forwarding the thread from Elena. Key asks from NorthView: sample CSV + 10k subset by Friday and a desire to see Redwood-hosted rerank vs their cloud endpoint.

Immediate asks for the infra team (Jordan):
- Can you run a micro-benchmark next week that mirrors the plan? Target: embedding dims 256/512/1024, HNSW vs IVF+PQ, retrieve top100 then rerank to top10. Use a 10k doc subset we control; we'll replace with NorthView data when they share it.
- Outputs I need: recall@1/5/10, end-to-end p95 latency, and a back-of-the-envelope per-1M-query cost estimate for each config.

Monica — please sync with Solutions Eng to confirm we can run an apples-to-apples cloud endpoint comparison (we'll need to reproduce their request pattern and include their current provider in the run). Also flag any SOC/NDAs we need before they share the subset.

Jordan — can you confirm availability for a short run Tues morning (11am PT) and whether you need a small box or can run on Dedicated dev cluster? If Dedicated dev cluster is required, I can request a 48h reservation.

I'll capture results in HubSpot and share the Drive folder once the run starts.

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

(Forwarded attachments: northview-embed-rerank-plan.pdf; sample-queries.csv)

source.subject: POC kickoff: coordinating hands-on windows across US/EU/APAC (routing/load tests)
messages:
From: Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

Hi Dev / Jordan,

Thanks again for the demo last week. NorthStar is ready to kickoff the POC next month. We want to run three hands-on working sessions (prompt review, routing checks, and a short load test) that need live access to our data plane for 90–120 minutes each. To make this effective for our global team, we need to rotate times so US, EU, and APAC colleagues can join at least one live slot.

Can you share the windows you can support the week of Aug 9? Tim and I sketched some preferred times but are flexible: mornings in the US (PDT), mid-afternoon in EU (CEST), and evening in APAC (SGT).

Attached is a draft agenda and a proposed routing/load-test checklist. We also included a quick Doodle so participants can mark availability.

Key asks from our side:
- Confirm three 90–120 minute windows (different days) with at least one rotating slot that moves ~8–10 hours each day so each region gets a live session.
- A named point-of-contact (SE) for prompt/script troubleshooting.
- A short pre-check (15 min) to validate permissions and NAT/firewall rules before each session.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call to align.

Samantha Li
Product Lead, Integrations
NorthStar Analytics

Attachments: POC-HandsOn-Agenda-v1.pdf, routing-load-test-plan.xlsx
From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>, Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 11:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

Hi Samantha — thanks for the clear asks and the agenda. We can absolutely run the three hands-on windows and will assign an SE for each session. Proposed approach from our side:

1) Week of Aug 9: propose Mon/Tue/Thu for the hands-on days (gives a buffer Wed for config changes).
2) Window rotation pattern: 0900 PDT / 1700 CEST / 0000 SGT (shift forward ~8 hrs each day) — exact times below.
3) Pre-checks: we’ll book 15-minute pre-check slots 48 hours ahead of each session.

Prelim slot options (please mark availability in the doodle link):
- Mon Aug 10: 09:00 PDT (16:00 CEST / 23:00 SGT) — focus: prompt tuning and prompt templates.
- Tue Aug 11: 17:00 CEST (08:00 PDT / 00:00 SGT next day) — focus: routing + model variant fallbacks.
- Thu Aug 13: 00:00 SGT (previous day 08:00 PDT / 17:00 CEST) — focus: load-testing and autoscaling behavior.

SEs: Jordan will own prompt tuning; Rafael will own routing/load tests. Laura will coordinate invites, pre-checks, and recording.

I attached a short Doodle placeholder (availability-doodle-link.txt) and our routing/load-test checklist updated with expected token volumes. If any of the proposed times are hard conflicts, tell us which region needs a swap and we’ll adapt.

Cheers,
Dev Patel
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

Attachments: availability-doodle-link.txt, routing-load-test-plan.xlsx
From: Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 11:41:00 -0400
Subject: Re: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

On Mon, Jul 12, 2026 at 11:05 AM Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com> wrote:
> Prelim slot options (please mark availability in the doodle link):
> - Mon Aug 10: 09:00 PDT (16:00 CEST / 23:00 SGT) — focus: prompt tuning and prompt templates.
> - Tue Aug 11: 17:00 CEST (08:00 PDT / 00:00 SGT next day) — focus: routing + model variant fallbacks.
> - Thu Aug 13: 00:00 SGT (previous day 08:00 PDT / 17:00 CEST) — focus: load-testing and autoscaling behavior.

Thanks — this looks great. Quick note: our APAC engineers prefer the APAC slot to be 19:00 SGT instead of midnight if possible. Can we shift the third session earlier by ~5 hours and keep the rotating pattern? If so, we'll update attendees.

Also, please confirm whether the pre-check requires a network window (we may need to open a NAT mapping for 1 hour).

—Tim
Principal Integration Engineer, NorthStar Analytics
From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>, Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 13:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

Hi Tim / Samantha — great point on APAC preferences. We can make the rotation work with a slightly different cadence that still guarantees live coverage for each region. Updated proposal:

- Mon Aug 10: 09:00 PDT / 16:00 CEST / 23:00 SGT — prompt tuning (Jordan lead).
- Tue Aug 11: 08:00 PDT / 15:00 CEST / 22:00 SGT — routing checks (Rafael lead).
- Thu Aug 13: 19:00 SGT / 10:00 PDT / 17:00 CEST — load test (Rafael + Jordan).

This keeps ~7–8 hour step shifts and moves the APAC slot to a more humane 19:00 SGT on Thu. Pre-checks: yes, we need a 15-minute ephemeral NAT rule for the load test and one prompt-sanity check. Laura, can you schedule the 15-min pre-checks for Aug 8 and Aug 12?

I’ll also prepare a short runbook for common prompt issues so the first session can be productive.

—Jordan
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>, Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Jul 2026 09:22:00 -0400
Subject: Re: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

Thanks all — I've updated the Doodle and blocked the proposed session times tentatively on our calendars. Finalized slots (provisional, pending your confirmations):

- Mon Aug 10 09:00 PDT / 16:00 CEST / 23:00 SGT — Prompt tuning (Jordan) — 90 min — Calendar invite sent.
- Tue Aug 11 08:00 PDT / 15:00 CEST / 22:00 SGT — Routing checks (Rafael) — 90 min — Calendar invite sent.
- Thu Aug 13 19:00 SGT / 10:00 PDT / 17:00 CEST — Load test (Rafael & Jordan) — 120 min — Calendar invite sent.

Pre-checks scheduled:
- Aug 8 08:45 PDT (15:45 CEST / 22:45 SGT) — prompt sanity check (15 min).
- Aug 12 09:45 PDT (16:45 CEST / 23:45 SGT) — network/NAT pre-check (15 min).

Attachments: final invites were attached when I sent the calendar invites (link included). Let me know by EOD Fri Jul 16 if we need to adjust any slots.

Laura Bennett
Program Coordinator, Redwood Inference
From: Samantha Li <samantha@northstar.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim O'Connor <tim.oconnor@northstar.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 11:02:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: POC kickoff + hands-on sessions — availability for rotating windows

Hi Laura / team — confirmed on our side. We've accepted the invites and added our APAC engineers to the Thu load test invite. One small request: can you record the prompt tuning session and share the runbook ahead of Mon Aug 10 so our product folks can prepare examples?

We will also need the temporary NAT window request filled (I'll raise it with infra once you confirm the exact minute window).

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Best,
Samantha Li
Product Lead, Integrations
NorthStar Analytics

messages:

To: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@medcore-health.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-12T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo readout + quick artifacts

Hi Aisha —

Thanks again for the time on today's demo. Quick recap from our side and the artifacts we promised:

1) High-level recommendation (based on the prompts you shared): two candidate model families — a 13B quantized variant for cost-sensitive real-time assists, and a 33B FP16 variant for higher-fidelity clinical summarization when latency budget is larger.
2) Performance samples: TPS and p50/p95 latency snapshots below (short summary) + links to full traces.
3) Prompt pack: curated prompt set adapted from your sample transcripts for regression checks.

Attachments included: redwood-verified-perf-pack-medcore.pdf (high-level perf profiles), prompt-set-medcore-v1.txt, inference-traces-medcore-0706.json (sanitized traces).

Action items / next steps I recommend:
- Your engineering/CTO: confirm pilot window (2–4 weeks) and preferred SLAs.
- We'll provision a sandbox with the 13B quantized model and the 33B FP16 model for side-by-side evaluation.
- Jordan will share the Redwood-verified performance pack and a short checklist for regression guardrails.

If you want, we can set a short 30-minute alignment call tomorrow to lock the pilot scope.

Best,
Chloe Martin
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
chloe@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0194

---

From: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@medcore-health.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@medcore-health.com>
Date: 2026-07-12T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo readout + quick artifacts

Thanks Chloe — this is helpful. Quick clarifying Qs from our side: 

- For the 13B quantized model, what p95 latencies should we expect at 8 TPS (text gen, avg prompt length ~400 tokens)?
- Any observed quality regressions vs the 33B when using your prompt pack? We want to avoid hallucination on medication fields.
- Pilot logistics: Can you confirm whether logs (for audit) include per-token timing and request traces? We need that for our security/compliance review.

Tom and I are available for a call tomorrow afternoon if that helps.

Appreciate it — Aisha
Aisha Patel
Head of AI Integrations, MedCore Health
aisha.patel@medcore-health.com | +1 (617) 555-0129

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@medcore-health.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@medcore-health.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-12T13:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo readout + quick artifacts

Hi Aisha —

Quick answers and pointers inline:

- 13B quantized @ 8 TPS (avg payload ~400 tokens): expect p50 ~85ms/token, p95 ~220ms/token on our Dedicated NVLink pool. End-to-end generation p95 for a 400-token completion is ~880ms. Those numbers map to ~8–10 RPS equivalent when pipelined; we include the full traces in inference-traces-medcore-0706.json.

- Quality regressions: In our internal sweep using your prompt pack the 13B Q8 profile produced similar entity recall for medication names (~95% precision on the curated set) but had slightly more partial matches on multi-drug instructions. The 33B FP16 variant consistently reduced partial-match rate by ~6 percentage points. That tradeoff is typical: stronger prefix handling/long-range dependencies on the larger model.

- Audit/logging: yes — sandbox provides per-request traces (timestamped), per-token timing, and a sanitized KV cache snapshot option for debugging. We can also stream logs into your Splunk/CloudWatch destination with IAM role.

Attachments: I attached the small "redwood-verified-perf-pack-medcore.pdf" (single-page executive summary) and a short checklist for regression tests (see attached). Full perf URL: https://app.redwood.ai/perf/medcore-MC-8891

If helpful, here's a suggested mini-pilot plan:
- Week 0: Provision sandbox + ingest sample prompt set (you provide 10k representative exchanges).
- Week 1–2: Run parallel evaluation across the two model variants; collect quality metrics (entity recall, hallucination rate), and perf traces at target TPS.
- Week 3: Review results; decide whether to proceed with Dedicated capacity or tune quantization profile.

I can jump on the call tomorrow at 2pm PT. Chloe — can you confirm?

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com | +1 (646) 555-0233

---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@medcore-health.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@medcore-health.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo readout + quick artifacts

Hi team —

Just adding capacity/ops notes from our side:

- If you want deterministic p95 under the 1s target for 400-token outputs, we recommend the 33B FP16 variant on Dedicated with the following config: 2x A100-80GBs in a colocated pool, batch size capped at 4, and prefix caching enabled. That configuration gives consistent p95 headroom at 8–12 TPS.

- Cost estimate (very rough): 33B FP16 Dedicated pilot (2 weeks) — estimated incremental infra cost ~$6,400; 13B Q8 sandbox run (2 weeks) — ~$1,100. We can provide a formal pilot quote if you want to proceed.

- Compliance note: our sandbox supports redaction hooks before logs leave the VPC and has HMAC-signed audit tokens for each trace.

Next steps we need from MedCore:
1) Confirm sample payloads (10k rows) and desired pilot start date window.
2) Decide whether to run both model variants in parallel or start with 13B Q8 and expand if quality delta warrants.

Happy to include our finance/SRE for the formal quote and onboarding if you confirm the pilot.

Thanks — Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Dedicated Products | Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0456

---

From: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@medcore-health.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
Cc: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@medcore-health.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T15:46:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo readout + quick artifacts

Thanks everyone — this is great context. We reviewed the perf pack and the short checklist. Aisha and I discussed and our preference is:

- Run the parallel pilot (both 13B Q8 and 33B FP16) so we can capture the quality delta under realistic load.
- Target pilot start: week of July 27 (gives our infra team two weeks to prepare sample payloads and security signoffs).
- Please send a formal pilot quote (Marcus) and a short onboarding doc for our infra (Jordan). We will also raise a HubSpot ticket for contract/procurement next week.

Appreciate the clear next steps — Tom
Tom Reynolds
VP Product & Platform, MedCore Health
tom.reynolds@medcore-health.com | +1 (617) 555-0177

source.subject: Nerve Center: Dedicated GPU pool sizing & isolation deep-dive — scheduling
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@novusai.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@novusai.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Dedicated GPU deep-dive (pool sizing, isolation)

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our infra and product teams want a focused technical deep-dive to validate pool sizing assumptions and tenant isolation options before we sign the PO. Key asks:
- Walkthrough of dedicated pool sizing model for sustained vs burst workloads
- Autoscaling behavior (policy knobs, cold-starts, queueing)
- Tenant isolation options and cross-tenant failure modes

Proposed duration: 75 minutes (technical + Q&A).
Proposed windows (PDT): Wed Jul 8 09:00–10:15, Thu Jul 9 14:00–15:15, Fri Jul 10 08:30–09:45.

I'll include our infra lead and a security SME if the slot works. Attaching our current capacity spreadsheet and an isolation checklist.

Attachments: Novus_capacity_model_v2.xlsx, tenant-isolation-checklist.pdf

Best,
Samir
Ops Lead, NovusAI
---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@novusai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: Dedicated GPU deep-dive (pool sizing, isolation)

Samir — thanks for the clear brief and the attachments. We can run a 75-minute session; prefer to keep it technical (eng + infra) with a short commercial recap at the end.

Availability from our side:
- Wed Jul 8 09:00–10:15 PDT — Jordan (me), Karthik, Kimberly available
- Thu Jul 9 14:00–15:15 PDT — Marissa also available (good for commercial Qs)

If you expect security/legal to attend, Thu 14:00 may be better. Can you confirm which window your team prefers and whether you'll need a recorded session?

Pre-reads: we'll push a short pre-read (redwood-pre-read.md) with the pool topology and autoscale knobs; attaching the Drive link. Please send any specific capacity targets or SLA numbers you want us to validate.

Jordan
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Calendar invite to follow after slot confirm — please indicate timezone for any additional attendees.

On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 08:12 AM Samir Patel <samir@novusai.com> wrote:
> Hi Jordan —
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week...

To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: Dedicated GPU deep-dive (pool sizing, isolation)

Putting a pin here — Thu 7/9 14:00 PDT works best for the buyer journey (gives us time to prep a one-slide SLO/cost summary).

Action items for our side before Thu:
- Karthik: prepare slides on autoscale policies and expected cold-start tail latencies
- Kimberly: draft a short tenant isolation appendix + emergency failover notes
- Jordan: confirm recording and invite list; sync with Samir for any added attendees from security/legal

Marissa
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@novusai.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Jul 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: Dedicated GPU deep-dive (pool sizing, isolation)

Jordan / Marissa — thanks. Thu 7/9 14:00 PDT is ideal. We'll have our infra lead (Ravi) and security SME (Alicia) on the call. Recording is fine.

Quick note on scope — please be prepared to:
- Validate our steady-state token/sec targets in Novus_capacity_model_v2.xlsx (tab: steady_state)
- Discuss the autoscaler behavior under sustained bursts (how queueing/backpressure is surfaced)
- Walk through tenant isolation test cases from the checklist (we'll call out three we want simulated)

Attaching the same files we sent earlier and a pointer to our SOC2 gap questionnaire — we may request SOC artifacts if the session looks good.

Attachments: Novus_capacity_model_v2.xlsx, tenant-isolation-checklist.pdf

Samir
Ops Lead, NovusAI

Confirming the calendar invite: Thu Jul 9, 2026 | 14:00–15:15 PDT / 17:00–18:15 EDT.

Agenda (75m):
- 0–10m: Introductions and objectives (Samir, Jordan)
- 10–35m: Pool topology & sizing model walkthrough (Karthik)
- 35–55m: Autoscaling behavior and failure modes (Karthik + Jordan)
- 55–65m: Tenant isolation scenarios & mitigation patterns (Kimberly)
- 65–75m: Commercial next steps / open Qs (Marissa)

source.subject: Kickoff scheduling: Week 0 hosting lanes & host bridge assignment
messages:
From: Jamie Park <jamie.park@arcadia-thera.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickoff — aligning Week 0 availability

Hi Connor / team,

Thanks again for yesterday's demo — the execs liked the low-latency profiler. We want to lock in a Week 0 kickoff that gets engineers, security, and our product sponsor all aligned. Can we target a 60-minute session during the week of July 13?

What we need from Redwood for the kickoff:
- Host bridge details and an ops lead for the session.
- A concise agenda (30m tech overview, 20m integration plan, 10m next steps).
- A short attendee roster (names + roles) to include in the calendar invite.

Attendees on our side so far: Jamie Park (PM), Sofia Martins (Infra lead), and one of our security engineers (TBD).

Prefer afternoons ET. Can you send a couple of slot options and a tentative agenda? Also please include any relevant join links.

— Jamie
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Jamie Park <jamie.park@arcadia-thera.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 17:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickoff — aligning Week 0 availability

Jamie — thanks, glad the demo landed well.

We can run the 60m Week 0 session. Proposed slots (all ET):
- Tue Jul 14, 2:00–3:00pm
- Wed Jul 15, 3:30–4:30pm
- Thu Jul 16, 1:00–2:00pm

Tentative agenda (we'll attach a one-pager):
1) 5m — Welcome & goals (Connor)
2) 30m — Runtime + integration checklist (Marissa + Olga)
3) 15m — Security posture + data flow items (Olga)
4) 10m — Next steps, owners, and timeline (Jordan)

Ops notes: Marissa will host the bridge; Olga will cover security artifacts and where to drop evidence post-meeting. If one of your security folks can't join, please share their email so we can record action items for follow-up.

I'll hold the tentative slots on my calendar for now. Which one works?

Connor
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jamie Park <jamie.park@arcadia-thera.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 18:21:00 -0400
Subject: RE: Kickoff — aligning Week 0 availability

Jamie / Connor — putting ops details inline.

I can host the bridge on Jul 15 if that works; I've booked a provisional 3:30–4:30pm ET slot on my end and attached a short Week0_Agenda_v1.pdf.

Attachments:
- Week0_Agenda_v1.pdf (agenda + prework links)
- Bridge-invite.ics (calendar invite we will send once slot confirmed)

Quick ops checklist for Arcadia before the call:
- Confirm one infra/security attendee with email for audit trail.
- Share any firewall or SSO allowlist entries at least 48h before meeting if you want us to demo within your environment.
- Add the following email addresses as meeting guests: marissa.cole@redwood.ai, olga.petrov@redwood.ai, jordan.blake@redwood.ai, connor.obrien@redwood.ai

If Jul 15 works, I will send the invite with the bridge link and attach the agenda + a one-page runbook.

— Marissa
From: Jamie Park <jamie.park@arcadia-thera.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sofia Martins <sofia.martins@arcadia-thera.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2026 09:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickoff — aligning Week 0 availability

Thanks — Jul 15 works for us. Confirming attendees now:
- Jamie Park (PM)
- Sofia Martins (Infra lead)
- Aaron Choi (security engineer) — will confirm by Monday; if he can't make it we'll share his contact/email.

Two quick asks:
1) Can you add a 10-minute slot at the end for Q&A aimed at our SREs? They tend to have follow-up questions about connection patterns.
2) Can you attach the SOC 2 evidence checklist or point to where we can access it ahead of time? Our security lead will want to review before the call.

If you can include both in the invite, we're good to go.

— Jamie
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Jamie Park <jamie.park@arcadia-thera.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 03 Jul 2026 09:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickoff — aligning Week 0 availability

Jamie — great, thanks for the quick turn. I've updated the agenda to include a 10m SRE Q&A at the end (total session now 70m; we will keep to 60m of content + 10m Q&A).

Action items:
- Marissa: finalize bridge + send calendar invite to the confirmed attendee list (please include the Week0_Agenda_v1.pdf and a link to SOC2 evidence folder).
- Olga: prep a 2-slide summary of the security artefacts we will reference (SOC 2 location + retention policy).
- Connor: capture owners and post-meeting timeline.

I'll send the confirmed calendar invite now and include both attachments + a link to the evidence folder. Looking forward to Jul 15 3:30–4:40pm ET.

Regards,
Jordan

source.subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Team —

Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. The deal is at signature (GF-3382) and procurement has signaled a hard gate: the pilot must demonstrate per-tenant QoS (latency SLO enforcement) and a short-term model residency/pinning guarantee for a 10-day activation window. They want this validated before committing the first monthly payment.

Key asks (from GF):
- Pin a model variant and provide a tenant-level QoS policy that limits end-to-end p95 latency to 250ms for a capped RPS.
- A short-term residency/pinning guarantee for the pilot (10 days) with rollback policy.
- A telemetry snapshot and acceptance test plan they can run during the pilot.

Why this matters: If we can offer a clear, timeboxed pin + QoS approach we keep GF in the deal (ACV $560k ARR annualized) and open the account to Dedicated expansion. They are sensitive to noisy neighbors and want deterministic performance for an internal trading workflow.

Proposed commercial trade: short-term SLA addendum for the 10-day pilot in exchange for commit to a Dedicated trial and a 6-month minimum if pilot meets acceptance criteria. I captured the mutual action plan and a compact requirements sheet (attached).

Asks for Product/Eng:
- Can we:
  1) Define a minimal implementation for tenant QoS enforcement that is safe to ship as a pilot (configurable policy + documented rollback).
  2) Approve an engineering timebox (estimate) and a tentative ETA for a pilot-ready config (non-invasive, feature-flagged path).

Timeline: GF expects pilot readiness week of 7/12. That gives ~2 weeks for an implementation plan + prioritization decision.

Requesting a quick sync to triage tradeoffs and document the minimal acceptance criteria. If this is a no-go, I need to tee up a fallback conversation with GF about a staged performance validation (paid POC).

Attaching: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf, galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

On Mon, 29 Jun 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. ...

Thanks for the clear summary, Ben. I reviewed the attached MAP and requirement sheet. A few clarifying points before I commit Eng budget/time: we need to be explicit about scope so we don't expand into a full multi-tenant QoS system for the pilot.

Suggested minimal scope (for pilot):
- Policy surface: route-level token that allows us to set a latency target and max concurrent requests per tenant (config only; enforcement via queueing + backpressure).
- Residency: a pinned worker pool assignment for the tenant for the 10-day window (best-effort on the first run; explicit fallback to shared pool if capacity drops).
- Observability: a telemetry snapshot + an automated acceptance test (RPS/latency runbook) that GF runs with our SRE.

Questions for Eng:
1) Marcus — rough estimate to implement a configurable route-level QoS token + basic backpressure enforcement (safe, feature-flagged path)?
2) Priyom — any existing infra that can support a short-term residency pin at the tenant layer without requiring a full tenancy isolation change?
3) Jordan — will the proposed approach require schema or SDK changes that client teams must pick up for the pilot?

If we can get rough estimates today, I can scope a short runbook and provide Ben an ETA to share with GF. If estimates look >2w, we should discuss the paid POC fallback.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Platform

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>, procurement@galaxyfinance.com
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Thanks, Laura — really helpful framing. Short answers inline to help prioritization: 

- Business impact: GF moves to signature upon receiving a deterministic pilot plan. If we miss the 7/12 readiness window they will push the deal and consider alternative vendors. This is a one-time strategic account with clear expansion potential.
- Commercial: GF has agreed to the short-term SLA addendum language; procurement is ready to sign once we confirm the technical approach and ETA.

Marcus / Priyom / Jordan — we need very rough T-shirt sizing today if possible (S / M / L + estimated engineering days). Laura has suggested a conservative minimal scope: route-level latency token + best-effort pinned worker pool + acceptance telemetry. Please reply with sizing and any blockers. If any of these are L (>3w), flag it and we will propose the paid POC alternative to GF (they indicated willingness).

Next steps I will take after your replies:
- Draft a short internal prioritization note for Eng leadership with expected impact and tradeoffs.
- If approved, coordinate a canary/feature-flag rollout and schedule the GF acceptance window (7/12 preferred).

Oliver / GF team — appreciate patience; pushing internally now and will confirm by EOD tomorrow.

Ben
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment stubs: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf (application/pdf); galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

Team —

Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. The deal is at signature (GF-3382) and procurement has signaled a hard gate: the pilot must demonstrate per-tenant QoS (latency SLO enforcement) and a short-term model residency/pinning guarantee for a 10-day activation window. They want this validated before committing the first monthly payment.

Key asks (from GF):
- Pin a model variant and provide a tenant-level QoS policy that limits end-to-end p95 latency to 250ms for a capped RPS.
- A short-term residency/pinning guarantee for the pilot (10 days) with rollback policy.
- A telemetry snapshot and acceptance test plan they can run during the pilot.

Why this matters: If we can offer a clear, timeboxed pin + QoS approach we keep GF in the deal (ACV $560k ARR annualized) and open the account to Dedicated expansion. They are sensitive to noisy neighbors and want deterministic performance for an internal trading workflow.

Proposed commercial trade: short-term SLA addendum for the 10-day pilot in exchange for commit to a Dedicated trial and a 6-month minimum if pilot meets acceptance criteria. I captured the mutual action plan and a compact requirements sheet (attached).

Asks for Product/Eng:
- Can we:
  1) Define a minimal implementation for tenant QoS enforcement that is safe to ship as a pilot (configurable policy + documented rollback).
  2) Approve an engineering timebox (estimate) and a tentative ETA for a pilot-ready config (non-invasive, feature-flagged path).

Timeline: GF expects pilot readiness week of 7/12. That gives ~2 weeks for an implementation plan + prioritization decision.

Requesting a quick sync to triage tradeoffs and document the minimal acceptance criteria. If this is a no-go, I need to tee up a fallback conversation with GF about a staged performance validation (paid POC).

Attaching: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf, galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

On Mon, 29 Jun 2026 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick context: Galaxy Finance (GF) is asking to finalize the pilot activation window for a production pilot that will go live in their US region. ...

Thanks for the clear summary, Ben. I reviewed the attached MAP and requirement sheet. A few clarifying points before I commit Eng budget/time: we need to be explicit about scope so we don't expand into a full multi-tenant QoS system for the pilot.

Suggested minimal scope (for pilot):
- Policy surface: route-level token that allows us to set a latency target and max concurrent requests per tenant (config only; enforcement via queueing + backpressure).
- Residency: a pinned worker pool assignment for the tenant for the 10-day window (best-effort on the first run; explicit fallback to shared pool if capacity drops).
- Observability: a telemetry snapshot + an automated acceptance test (RPS/latency runbook) that GF runs with our SRE.

Questions for Eng:
1) Marcus — rough estimate to implement a configurable route-level QoS token + basic backpressure enforcement (safe, feature-flagged path)?
2) Priyom — any existing infra that can support a short-term residency pin at the tenant layer without requiring a full tenancy isolation change?
3) Jordan — will the proposed approach require schema or SDK changes that client teams must pick up for the pilot?

If we can get rough estimates today, I can scope a short runbook and provide Ben an ETA to share with GF. If estimates look >2w, we should discuss the paid POC fallback.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Platform


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@galaxyfinance.com>, procurement@galaxyfinance.com
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Galaxy Finance pilot — tenant QoS pin + activation window request

Thanks, Laura — really helpful framing. Short answers inline to help prioritization: 

- Business impact: GF moves to signature upon receiving a deterministic pilot plan. If we miss the 7/12 readiness window they will push the deal and consider alternative vendors. This is a one-time strategic account with clear expansion potential.
- Commercial: GF has agreed to the short-term SLA addendum language; procurement is ready to sign once we confirm the technical approach and ETA.

Marcus / Priyom / Jordan — we need very rough T-shirt sizing today if possible (S / M / L + estimated engineering days). Laura has suggested a conservative minimal scope: route-level latency token + best-effort pinned worker pool + acceptance telemetry. Please reply with sizing and any blockers. If any of these are L (>3w), flag it and we will propose the paid POC alternative to GF (they indicated willingness).

Next steps I will take after your replies:
- Draft a short internal prioritization note for Eng leadership with expected impact and tradeoffs.
- If approved, coordinate a canary/feature-flag rollout and schedule the GF acceptance window (7/12 preferred).

Oliver / GF team — appreciate patience; pushing internally now and will confirm by EOD tomorrow.

Ben
Senior Account Exec, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment stubs: GF-3382_MutualActionPlan_v1.pdf (application/pdf); galaxy_mvp_req_sheet.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
