messages:
From: Alex Martin <alex.martin@acme-sec.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: SDLC provenance playbook + CI/CD controls (SIG/CAIQ follow-up)

Hi Aisha,

Following our SIG/CAIQ review, the assessor team needs a concise evidence packet focused on secure SDLC provenance and CI/CD controls for the past 12 months. Can you provide the following artifacts (redacted where necessary) or point us to a shared folder?

- The SDLC provenance/playbook document that describes code review gating, PR approval policy, release gating, and change board workflows.
- Examples of PR approval records (sample PRs showing approvals + reviewer names + timestamps).
- CI/CD run extracts for at least three representative production deployments (job names, timestamps, exit statuses, artifact hashes).
- Evidence of automated secret scanning (policy config, sample scan runs showing blocked commits).
- Change management records for emergency and normal releases (approvals list or minutes).
- A short note describing ephemeral credential usage (ephemeral tokens, short-lived keys, KMS integration).

Target needed by: EOD Thu, Jul 1, 2026 (assessor lead is traveling Friday). If any of the items require additional redaction or we should accept a manager-signed attestation instead of full logs, let me know.

Thanks,
Alex Martin
Senior Assessor — Acme Security
assessor@acme-sec.com

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martin <alex.martin@acme-sec.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 12:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: SDLC provenance playbook + CI/CD controls (SIG/CAIQ follow-up)

On Mon, Jun 29, 2026 at 9:12 AM Alex Martin <alex.martin@acme-sec.com> wrote:
> Following our SIG/CAIQ review, the assessor team needs a concise evidence packet focused on secure SDLC provenance and CI/CD controls for the past 12 months.

Hi Alex — thanks for the clarity and the deadline. We'll assemble a compact packet and drop a folder link. High-level plan and what we'll include below.

What we'll provide by EOD Thu, Jul 1:
- "redwood_playbook_sdlc_provenance_v1.pdf" — our SDLC provenance/playbook that covers PR gating, code review expectations, canary rollouts, and change board roles.
- "sample_pr_approval_export.csv" — redacted PR metadata (PR id, author, approver, timestamps) — 3 samples across different teams.
- "ci_job_run_excerpt-202605.csv" — selected CI job runs (job name, start/end, status, artifact hash) for three prod deployments.
- "secrets-scan-policy.yaml" + one sample run showing a blocked commit (redacted path).
- "change-board-minutes-20260429.pdf" — minutes from a normal change board and brief notes on emergency workflow.
- Short answer doc about ephemeral credentials + KMS usage.

Irene, Jordan, Grace — I've CC'd you to coordinate the exports and any redaction. Irene: can you produce the secrets-scan sample and playbook section describing the scanner enforcement? Jordan: please pull the CI job run extracts from BuildWave and redact the artifact payloads but keep hashes. Grace: please pull PR approval samples.

Related artifacts will be uploaded to the drive link below (I'll share once the initial packet is ready). If we need to provide manager attestation instead of raw logs, please flag; Alex said that's an allowable alternative. Also linking HubSpot ticket HS-55902 for tracking.

Drive (share pending): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB_example_playbook_folder
HubSpot: HS-55902

Thanks — will follow up with attachments.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Customer Reliability (on behalf of Security Response)
Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Evidence collection tasks for Acme assessor (HS-55902)

Team — quick tasking, timelines below. Keeping this internal to coordinate PII/redaction.

Tasks (owners + deliverables):
- Irene (Security Engineer) — provide secrets-scan-policy.yaml and one representative scan run that shows a blocked commit. Filename: "secrets-scan-policy.yaml" and "secrets_scan_sample-20260523.txt". Redact user IDs and repo paths (replace with <redacted-repo>). Deadline: Wed, Jun 30 17:00 PT.
- Jordan (Platform) — export CI job runs from BuildWave for three production deployments (IDs: prod-20260212, prod-20260327, prod-20260518). Export should include job name, start/end timestamps, status, and artifact SHA256 hashes. Filename: "ci_job_run_excerpt-202605.csv". DO NOT include binary artifacts; include artifact hash only. Please also export deploy approval references (Jira keys) — e.g., JIRA-3456. Deadline: Wed, Jun 30 18:00 PT.
- Grace (Eng Mgmt) — select three PRs (one infra, one runtime, one feature) and export approval metadata (PR id, author, approvers, timestamps). Redact email addresses to initials if needed. Filename: "sample_pr_approval_export.csv". Deadline: Wed, Jun 30 18:00 PT.
- Aisha — I'll assemble the playbook doc and draft a short attestation if we opt to provide signed confirmation in lieu of raw logs. Filename: "redwood_playbook_sdlc_provenance_v1.pdf". Deadline: Thu, Jul 1 12:00 PT.

Notes/constraints:
- CI provider retention: BuildWave keeps full run logs for 12 months; exports may include IPs—redact any extraneous network info.
- Secrets scanner: we use SentinelScan; policy files are in the config repo. Ensure we strip webhook tokens and KMS ARNs.
- Change board minutes: Jordan, pull the PDF from the board drive and mark sensitive customer names as <customer-redacted>.

If anything looks like it will slip, escalate to me immediately. Once we have all artifacts, I'll bundle and share the drive link with Acme.

Thanks,
Aisha

From: Alex Martin <alex.martin@acme-sec.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: SDLC provenance playbook + CI/CD controls (SIG/CAIQ follow-up)

Thanks Aisha — that plan sounds good. Two quick clarifications before you upload: 

1) For the CI job run extracts, we need artifact hashes at the job level (SHA256 or SHA1 accepted) so we can map to release artifacts in our sampling. If you can include the hash column (redacted artifact path okay), that will suffice.
2) For the secrets-scan sample, if it’s not possible to include a blocked commit (due to legal/partner data), a manager-signed attestation describing the enforcement and a redacted screenshot of a blocked run is acceptable.

If you can confirm those two points in your packet, we'll be able to close this line item. If helpful, I'm available Wed Jul 1 at 11:00 PT for a 20m call.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Regards,
Alex Martin
Senior Assessor — Acme Security
assessor@acme-sec.com

source.subject: Operator witnessing tiers and proposed exhibit language for private deployment
messages:
From: Anika Rao <anika.rao@medisyshealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Operator witnessing tiers and evidence path for private deployment

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we start to firm up the order form for the private (VPC) deployment, procurement and our security team asked for clarification on three items so we can scope the MSA exhibits correctly:

1) Operator witnessing options: we need discrete definitions for (a) remote witnessed access, (b) on‑site witnessed access, and (c) escrow‑lite (sealed snapshot stewarded by a third party). For each option we need expected timelines, who's responsible for witness coordination, and the minimum artifacts Medisys receives to validate operator capability.

2) Credential and KMS boundary: who retains custody of operator creds during a witnessed session? Can an operator be scoped to ephemeral credentials that expire at session close, and do we need a signed attestation?

3) Proof window and evidence delivery: procurement wants a sample exhibit describing the proof‑of‑access window (how long witnessing is available), what logs/artifacts are delivered, and retention limits.

I've attached our internal Evidence Request Form to show the artifacts we typically collect. Can you point us to the order‑form language or an exhibit draft we can share with legal/procurement? Also please include any incremental cost or scheduling impact for on‑site versus remote witnessed access.

Best,
